## בִּאָה (id: 000810000000000)

בִּאָה
======

בִּאָה (H0872)

noun f · בוא

Constructions

entrance \= place where one enters a building or structure

[EZK 8:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek8:5)

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 8:5


## בְּאוּשִׁים (id: 000811000000000)

בְּאוּשִׁים
===========

בְּאוּשִׁים (H0891) — בָּאֻשׁ

noun m · באשׁ

Plants

rotten fruit, stinking weeds \= undesirable plant product, probably bad\-smelling and inedible

[ISA 5:2](https://ref.ly/Isa5:2)1 · [ISA 5:4](https://ref.ly/Isa5:4)1

1 ISA 5:2 · ISA 5:4 NIV "bad fruit" GNT "sour grapes" CEV "bitter grapes" \| RSV NJPS NJB NRSV REB "wild grapes"

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 5:2; Isaiah 5:4


## בִּאישׁ (id: 000812000000000)

בִּאישׁ
=======

בִּאישׁ (A0873)

adj

Evil

evil, wicked \= state in which humans are wicked and immoral in character

[EZR 4:12](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:12)

1 EZR 4:12 MT\-K ובאישׁתא \| MT\-Q וּבִישְׁתָּא

Reinier de Blois

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 4:12


## באר (id: 000813000000000)

באר
===

באר (H0874)

verb

Simple

to make clear \= action by which some content is expressed in words or written in such a way that it may be simple to understand

[DEU 1:5](https://ref.ly/Deut1:5) · [DEU 27:8](https://ref.ly/Deut27:8) · [HAB 2:2](https://ref.ly/Hab2:2)

Sarah Lind; Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 1:5; Deuteronomy 27:8; Habakkuk 2:2


## בְּאֵר (id: 000814000000000)

בְּאֵר
======

בְּאֵר (H0875 · H0876 · H0883 · H0884)

noun f · בֹּור · בַּיִר · באר · בְּאֵר אֵילִים · בְּאֵר לַחַי רֹאִי · בְּאֵר שֶׁבַע · בְּאֵרָא · בְּאֵרָה · בְּאֵרֹות · בְּאֵרִי · בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי־יַעֲקָן · בַּעֲלַת בְּאֵר

Waterbodies

well, watering place, spring \= hole in the ground that reaches underground water; ◄ usually manmade, dug out of the ground, or hewn from rock, but may also be naturally\-occurring; ► used for accessing, collecting and storing water; used by shepherds as a source of water for their animals; ≈ regarded as a source of life because of its water; sometimes used as a metaphor for a woman, possibly based on the physical resemblance of the well to the vagina and its connotation as a source of life

[GEN 16:14](https://ref.ly/Gen16:14) · [GEN 21:19](https://ref.ly/Gen21:19) · [GEN 21:25](https://ref.ly/Gen21:25) · [GEN 21:30](https://ref.ly/Gen21:30) · [GEN 24:11](https://ref.ly/Gen24:11) · [GEN 24:20](https://ref.ly/Gen24:20) · [GEN 26:15](https://ref.ly/Gen26:15) · [GEN 26:18](https://ref.ly/Gen26:18) · [GEN 26:19](https://ref.ly/Gen26:19) · [GEN 26:20](https://ref.ly/Gen26:20) · [GEN 26:21](https://ref.ly/Gen26:21) · [GEN 26:22](https://ref.ly/Gen26:22) · [GEN 26:25](https://ref.ly/Gen26:25) · [GEN 26:32](https://ref.ly/Gen26:32) · [GEN 29:2](https://ref.ly/Gen29:2) · [GEN 29:2](https://ref.ly/Gen29:2) · [GEN 29:2](https://ref.ly/Gen29:2) · [GEN 29:3](https://ref.ly/Gen29:3) · [GEN 29:3](https://ref.ly/Gen29:3) · [GEN 29:8](https://ref.ly/Gen29:8) · [GEN 29:10](https://ref.ly/Gen29:10) · [EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15) · [NUM 20:17](https://ref.ly/Num20:17) · [NUM 21:16](https://ref.ly/Num21:16) · [NUM 21:17](https://ref.ly/Num21:17) · [NUM 21:18](https://ref.ly/Num21:18) · [NUM 21:22](https://ref.ly/Num21:22) · [2SA 17:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:18)1 · [2SA 17:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:19)1 · [2SA 17:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:21)1 · [PRO 5:15](https://ref.ly/Prov5:15) · [SNG 4:15](https://ref.ly/Song4:15)

Landforms

**Collocation:** בֶּאֱרֹת חֵמָר

tar pit, bitumen pit \= hole in the ground where crude oil seeps to the surface from inside the earth

[GEN 14:10](https://ref.ly/Gen14:10) · [GEN 14:10](https://ref.ly/Gen14:10)

Landforms

pit \= naturally occurring depression, hole or pit, usually containing a substance in liquid form; ≈ a metaphor for death, based on the physical resemblance of a pit to a grave, on the danger of falling into a pit, or being drowned by water in it, and on the depth of a pit recalling the underworld place of the dead

[PSA 55:24](https://ref.ly/Ps55:24) · [PSA 69:16](https://ref.ly/Ps69:16) · [PRO 23:27](https://ref.ly/Prov23:27)

name · באר

Names of Locations

Beer \= location with a well; ◄ in Sinai peninsula; ► the Israelites encamped there on their way from Egypt to Canaan

[NUM 21:16](https://ref.ly/Num21:16)

Names of Locations

Beer \= town, possibly to be identified with Beeroth; ◄ located around ten kilometers north of Jerusalem; territory of Benjamin

[JDG 9:21](https://ref.ly/Judg9:21)

Names of Locations

**Collocation:** בְּאֵר שֶׁבַע

Beersheba \= town; ◄ located in the Negeb, at the southern border of Judah; ► used esp. in combination with Dan at the northern border of Israel to refer to all Israel

[GEN 21:14](https://ref.ly/Gen21:14) · [GEN 21:31](https://ref.ly/Gen21:31) · [GEN 21:32](https://ref.ly/Gen21:32) · [GEN 21:33](https://ref.ly/Gen21:33) · [GEN 22:19](https://ref.ly/Gen22:19) · [GEN 22:19](https://ref.ly/Gen22:19) · [GEN 26:23](https://ref.ly/Gen26:23) · [GEN 26:33](https://ref.ly/Gen26:33) · [GEN 28:10](https://ref.ly/Gen28:10) · [GEN 46:1](https://ref.ly/Gen46:1) · [GEN 46:5](https://ref.ly/Gen46:5) · [JOS 15:28](https://ref.ly/Josh15:28) · [JOS 19:2](https://ref.ly/Josh19:2) · [JDG 20:1](https://ref.ly/Judg20:1) · [1SA 3:20](https://ref.ly/1Sam3:20) · [1SA 8:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam8:2) · [2SA 3:10](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:10) · [2SA 17:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:11) · [2SA 24:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:2) · [2SA 24:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:7) · [2SA 24:15](https://ref.ly/2Sam24:15) · [1KI 5:5](https://ref.ly/1Kgs5:5) · [1KI 19:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:3) · [2KI 12:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs12:2) · [2KI 23:8](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:8) · [1CH 4:28](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:28) · [1CH 21:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:2) · [2CH 19:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:4) · [2CH 24:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr24:1) · [2CH 30:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr30:5) · [NEH 11:27](https://ref.ly/Neh11:27) · [NEH 11:30](https://ref.ly/Neh11:30) · [AMO 5:5](https://ref.ly/Amos5:5) · [AMO 8:14](https://ref.ly/Amos8:14)

1 2SA 17:18 · 2SA 17:19 · 2SA 17:21 Dalman (\|iArbeit und Sitte in Palästina\|i\*, V 3 \[Gütersloh 1928–42], 200\) thinks this must be a place for storing grain, since the two men hide in it.

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 16:14; Genesis 21:19; Genesis 21:25; Genesis 21:30; Genesis 24:11; Genesis 24:20; Genesis 26:15; Genesis 26:18; Genesis 26:19; Genesis 26:20; Genesis 26:21; Genesis 26:22; Genesis 26:25; Genesis 26:32; Genesis 29:2; Genesis 29:3; Genesis 29:8; Genesis 29:10; Exodus 2:15; Numbers 20:17; Numbers 21:16; Numbers 21:17; Numbers 21:18; Numbers 21:22; 2 Samuel 17:18; 2 Samuel 17:19; 2 Samuel 17:21; Proverbs 5:15; Song of Songs 4:15; Genesis 14:10; Psalms 55:24; Psalms 69:16; Proverbs 23:27; Judges 9:21; Genesis 21:14; Genesis 21:31; Genesis 21:32; Genesis 21:33; Genesis 22:19; Genesis 26:23; Genesis 26:33; Genesis 28:10; Genesis 46:1; Genesis 46:5; Joshua 15:28; Joshua 19:2; Judges 20:1; 1 Samuel 3:20; 1 Samuel 8:2; 2 Samuel 3:10; 2 Samuel 17:11; 2 Samuel 24:2; 2 Samuel 24:7; 2 Samuel 24:15; 1 Kings 5:5; 1 Kings 19:3; 2 Kings 12:2; 2 Kings 23:8; 1 Chronicles 4:28; 1 Chronicles 21:2; 2 Chronicles 19:4; 2 Chronicles 24:1; 2 Chronicles 30:5; Nehemiah 11:27; Nehemiah 11:30; Amos 5:5; Amos 8:14

## בְּאֵר לַחַי רֹאִי (id: 000816000000000)

==================

בְּאֵר לַחַי רֹאִי (H0883) — לַחַי רֹאִי

name · בְּאֵר · חַי · ראה

Names of Locations

Beer\-Lahai\-Roi \= location, perhaps a shrine; ◄ between Kadesh and Bered in the Negeb; ► named by Hagar after a messenger of God spoke with her; Isaac later settled there

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 16:14; Genesis 24:62; Genesis 25:11

## בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי יַעֲקָן (id: 000822000000000)

בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי יַעֲקָן
=======================

בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי יַעֲקָן (H0885) — בְּאֵרֹת בְּנֵי־יַעֲקָן

name · בְּאֵר · בֵּן · יַעֲקָן

Names of Locations

Beeroth\-bene\-jaakan \= location, also known as Bene\-Jaakan; ◄ in Sinai peninsula, between Moserah and Hor\-Haggidgad; ► the Israelites encamped there on their way from Egypt to Canaan

[DEU 10:6](https://ref.ly/Deut10:6)1

1 DEU 10:6 GNT, CEV, and NIV translate the components of the name instead of a place name: the wells belonging to the people/descendants of Jaakan. Since בְּנֵי־יַעֲקָן seems to be used as a place name in Num 33:31,32, "wells of Bene\-jaakan" is also a possibility.

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 10:6

## באשׁ (id: 000824000000000)

באשׁ
====

באשׁ (H0887 · A0888)

verb · בְּאוּשִׁים · בְּאֹשׁ · בָּאְשָׁה

Smell

to become foul, to stink \= process by which objects such as dead animals, plants, or wounds give off a foul odor; ≈ considered repulsive

[EXO 7:18](https://ref.ly/Exod7:18) · [EXO 7:21](https://ref.ly/Exod7:21) · [EXO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Exod8:10) · [EXO 16:20](https://ref.ly/Exod16:20) · [EXO 16:24](https://ref.ly/Exod16:24) · [PSA 38:6](https://ref.ly/Ps38:6) · [ISA 50:2](https://ref.ly/Isa50:2)

Smell

to make stink \= process by which dead animals cause something else to give off a foul odor; ≈ considered repulsive

[ECC 10:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:1)

Hate

to become repulsive \= process by which humans become repulsive or loathsome to someone else ◄ as a result of antagonistic actions ► resulting in enmity between the parties involved

[1SA 13:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:4) · [1SA 27:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:12) · [1SA 27:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam27:12) · [2SA 10:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:6) · [2SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21) · [1CH 19:6](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:6) · [PRO 13:5](https://ref.ly/Prov13:5) · [ISA 30:5](https://ref.ly/Isa30:5)1

Hate

to make repulsive \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to be or become repulsive or loathsome to someone else ◄ as a result of antagonistic actions ► resulting in enmity between the parties involved

[GEN 34:30](https://ref.ly/Gen34:30)

Hate

**Collocation:** באשׁ רֵיחַ

to spoil someone's smell \> reputation \= literally: to cause (someone's) odor to give off a bad smell in the eyes of someone else; hence: \= process by which humans cause other humans to become repulsive or loathsome in the eyes of someone else ◄ as a result of antagonistic actions ► resulting in enmity between the parties involved

[EXO 5:21](https://ref.ly/Exod5:21)

Grieve

to be sad, to be upset \= state in which humans experience distress over something

[DAN 6:15](https://ref.ly/Dan6:15)

1 ISA 30:5 MT \| RSV NRSV ESV "comes to shame" NIV "be put to shame" NJPS "come to shame" NJB "be disappointed" \= בושׁ

Sarah Lind

* **Associated Passages:** Exodus 7:18; Exodus 7:21; Exodus 8:10; Exodus 16:20; Exodus 16:24; Psalms 38:6; Isaiah 50:2; Ecclesiastes 10:1; 1 Samuel 13:4; 1 Samuel 27:12; 2 Samuel 10:6; 2 Samuel 16:21; 1 Chronicles 19:6; Proverbs 13:5; Isaiah 30:5; Genesis 34:30; Exodus 5:21; Daniel 6:15

======

בָּבֶל (H0894 · A0895)

name

Names of Locations

בָּבְלָי
========

adj

## בַּג (id: 000832000000000)

בַּג
====

בַּג (H0897)

noun m

Possess

plunder, booty \= objects taken from a defeated enemy in war

1 EZK 25:7 MT\-K לבג \| MT\-Q לְבַז see בַּז

Enio R. Mueller



## בגד (id: 000833000000000)

בגד
===

בגד (H0898)

verb · בֶּגֶד · בֹּגְדֹות · בָּגֹוד

Unfaithful

to act unfaithfully, to be treacherous, to deal treacherously, to break faith, to betray \= pattern of behavior by which humans act in a way which shows a lack of truthfulness and commitment, both in words and deeds

## בַּד (id: 000840000000000)

בַּד
====

בַּד (H0905 · H0906 · H0907)

noun m

Cloth

linen \= woven material ◄ made from the stems of the flax plant; absorbs perspiration better than many other types of cloth; ► used for production of clothes; worn by priests and supernatural beings; ≈ associated with quality, strength, and coolness

## בדא (id: 000841000000000)

בדא
===

בדא (H0908)

verb · בַּד

Think

**Collocation:** בדא מִלֵּב

to choose, to devise, to invent, to make up literally: to devise from (one's own) heart; hence: \= to make a decision based on one's own thinking only, and without being informed by a reality outside of oneself

[1KI 12:33](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:33) · [NEH 6:8](https://ref.ly/Neh6:8)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** 1 Kings 12:33; Nehemiah 6:8

בדד
===

בדד (H0909)

verb · בַּד · בָּדָד

to be alone, to be isolated, to be a straggler \= state of being apart from others belonging to the same group

[PSA 102:8](https://ref.ly/Ps102:8) · [ISA 14:31](https://ref.ly/Isa14:31) · [HOS 8:9](https://ref.ly/Hos8:9)

Enio R. Mueller

Tools

===

בדל (H0914)

verb · בָּדָל · בָּדִיל · מִבְדָּלָה

Non\-Whole

to separate, to sever \= action by which part of an entity is separated from the rest, either by detaching it completely it or by creating a kind of barrier (visible or invisible) between the selected part and the rest of the entity

[GEN 1:4](https://ref.ly/Gen1:4) · [GEN 1:6](https://ref.ly/Gen1:6) · [GEN 1:7](https://ref.ly/Gen1:7) · [GEN 1:14](https://ref.ly/Gen1:14) · [GEN 1:18](https://ref.ly/Gen1:18) · [EXO 26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:33) · [LEV 1:17](https://ref.ly/Lev1:17) · [LEV 5:8](https://ref.ly/Lev5:8) · [EZK 42:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek42:20)

Dissociate

to separate oneself, to defect, to sequester oneself \= action by which humans disassociate themselves from a group of people

[NUM 16:21](https://ref.ly/Num16:21) · [1CH 12:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr12:9) · [EZR 6:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:21) · [EZR 9:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:1) · [EZR 10:11](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:11) · [EZR 10:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:16)1 · [NEH 9:2](https://ref.ly/Neh9:2) · [NEH 10:29](https://ref.ly/Neh10:29)

Dissociate

to separate, to set apart, to single out, to exclude, to detach \= action by which part of a group is dissociated from the rest

[LEV 20:24](https://ref.ly/Lev20:24) · [LEV 20:25](https://ref.ly/Lev20:25) · [LEV 20:26](https://ref.ly/Lev20:26) · [NUM 8:14](https://ref.ly/Num8:14) · [NUM 16:9](https://ref.ly/Num16:9) · [DEU 4:41](https://ref.ly/Deut4:41) · [DEU 10:8](https://ref.ly/Deut10:8) · [DEU 19:2](https://ref.ly/Deut19:2) · [DEU 19:7](https://ref.ly/Deut19:7) · [DEU 29:20](https://ref.ly/Deut29:20) · [1KI 8:53](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:53) · [1CH 23:13](https://ref.ly/1Chr23:13) · [1CH 25:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr25:1) · [2CH 25:10](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:10)1 · [EZR 8:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra8:24) · [EZR 10:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:8) · [EZR 10:16](https://ref.ly/Ezra10:16)1 · [NEH 13:3](https://ref.ly/Neh13:3) · [ISA 56:3](https://ref.ly/Isa56:3) · [ISA 56:3](https://ref.ly/Isa56:3) · [ISA 59:2](https://ref.ly/Isa59:2) · [EZK 39:14](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:14)

Know

to distinguish \= state in which humans recognize the difference between entities on the basis of one or more of their characterteristics and avoid what does not meet the standard

[LEV 10:10](https://ref.ly/Lev10:10) · [LEV 11:47](https://ref.ly/Lev11:47) · [LEV 20:25](https://ref.ly/Lev20:25) · [EZK 22:26](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:26)

1 EZR 10:16 · 2CH 25:10 The preposition ל preceding the direct object in this phrase is probably an aramaism.

Reinier de Blois

## בהל (id: 000860000000000)

בהל
===

בהל (H0926 · A0927)

verb · בֶּהָלָה

Urge

to be quick, to be hasty, to be rash, sudden \= action by which humans or divine beings act quickly

[EST 6:14](https://ref.ly/Esth6:14) · [PRO 28:22](https://ref.ly/Prov28:22) · [ECC 5:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl5:1) · [ECC 7:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl7:9) · [ECC 8:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:3)1 · [DAN 2:25](https://ref.ly/Dan2:25) · [DAN 3:24](https://ref.ly/Dan3:24) · [DAN 6:20](https://ref.ly/Dan6:20) · [ZEP 1:18](https://ref.ly/Zeph1:18)2

Urge

to make (someone) hurry \= action by which humans or divine beings cause (other) humans to act quickly

[2CH 35:21](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:21) · [EST 8:14](https://ref.ly/Esth8:14)

Urge

to speed up \= action by which humans cause something to happen without delay

[EST 2:9](https://ref.ly/Esth2:9) · [PRO 20:21](https://ref.ly/Prov20:21)3

Move

to hurry (someone) out \= action by which humans cause someone else to move quickly from a certain location

[2CH 26:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr26:20)

verb · בֶּהָלָה

Afraid

to be in anguish, to be dismayed \= state in which humans experience a great fear combined with severe distress, often accompanied by physical symptoms, such trembling and pain

[GEN 45:3](https://ref.ly/Gen45:3) · [EXO 15:15](https://ref.ly/Exod15:15) · [JDG 20:41](https://ref.ly/Judg20:41) · [1SA 28:21](https://ref.ly/1Sam28:21) · [2SA 4:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:1) · [JOB 4:5](https://ref.ly/Job4:5) · [JOB 21:6](https://ref.ly/Job21:6) · [JOB 23:15](https://ref.ly/Job23:15) · [PSA 6:3](https://ref.ly/Ps6:3) · [PSA 6:4](https://ref.ly/Ps6:4) · [PSA 6:11](https://ref.ly/Ps6:11) · [PSA 30:8](https://ref.ly/Ps30:8) · [PSA 48:6](https://ref.ly/Ps48:6) · [ECC 8:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:3)1 · [ECC 8:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl8:3)1 · [ISA 13:8](https://ref.ly/Isa13:8) · [ISA 21:3](https://ref.ly/Isa21:3) · [JER 51:32](https://ref.ly/Jer51:32) · [EZK 7:27](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:27) · [EZK 26:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:18) · [DAN 5:9](https://ref.ly/Dan5:9)

Afraid

to terrify, to dismay \= action by which humans, divine beings, or certain events cause (other) humans to experience a state of great fear combined with severe distress, often accompanied by physical symptoms, such as trembling and pain

[2CH 32:18](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:18) · [EZR 4:4](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:4) · [JOB 22:10](https://ref.ly/Job22:10) · [JOB 23:16](https://ref.ly/Job23:16) · [PSA 2:5](https://ref.ly/Ps2:5) · [PSA 83:16](https://ref.ly/Ps83:16) · [PSA 83:18](https://ref.ly/Ps83:18) · [PSA 90:7](https://ref.ly/Ps90:7) · [PSA 104:29](https://ref.ly/Ps104:29) · [DAN 4:2](https://ref.ly/Dan4:2) · [DAN 4:16](https://ref.ly/Dan4:16) · [DAN 4:16](https://ref.ly/Dan4:16) · [DAN 5:6](https://ref.ly/Dan5:6) · [DAN 5:10](https://ref.ly/Dan5:10) · [DAN 7:15](https://ref.ly/Dan7:15) · [DAN 7:28](https://ref.ly/Dan7:28) · [DAN 11:44](https://ref.ly/Dan11:44)

## בְּהֵמֹות (id: 000863000000000)

בְּהֵמֹות
=========

בְּהֵמֹות (H0930)

noun m · בְּהֵמָה

Wild Animals

Behemoth, elephant, hippopotamus \= a large wild animal, sometimes identified with the hippopotamus but more likely to denote the elephant or a mythical monster

[JOB 40:15](https://ref.ly/Job40:15)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Job 40:15


## בֹּהֶן (id: 000864000000000)

בֹּהֶן
======

בֹּהֶן (H0931 · H0932) — בְּהֹון

noun f

thumb, big toe \= short, thick, inner digit of human hand or foot

## בֹּהַק (id: 000865000000000)

בֹּהַק
======

בֹּהַק (H0933)

noun m

Impact

leucodermia, vitiligo \= condition causing white spots on the body; ► not resulting in ritual uncleanness as opposed to several other skin diseases

[LEV 13:39](https://ref.ly/Lev13:39)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 13:39


## בַּהֶרֶת (id: 000866000000000)

בַּהֶרֶת
========

בַּהֶרֶת (H0934)

noun f · בָּהִיר

Impact

spot \= small area in the skin that looks different from the rest and could possibly be a sign of an infectious skin disease which would make the person affected ritually unclean

בוא

Arrange

**Collocation:** בוא בַּמָּצֹור

to be besieged literally: to come in siege; hence: \= process by which a town is being surrounded by a group of people ► for purposes of war

[DEU 20:19](https://ref.ly/Deut20:19) · [2KI 24:10](https://ref.ly/2Kgs24:10) · [2KI 25:2](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:2) · [JER 52:5](https://ref.ly/Jer52:5)

Associate

to be reckenoned (among a group), to be included (in a group) literally: to come (to a group); hence: \= state in which humans are considered part of a certain group

[1CH 11:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:21) · [1CH 11:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:25) · [JOB 3:6](https://ref.ly/Job3:6)

Associate

to marry literally: to enter (a woman's room); hence: \= action by which a man takes a woman as his lawful wife

[2SA 17:25](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:25)

Associate

**Collocation:** בוא בִּבְרִית

to enter into a covenant literally: (people) enter into a covenant; hence: \= action by which a group of people makes a solemn agreement among themselves to pursue a certain goal

[2CH 15:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr15:12) · [JER 34:10](https://ref.ly/Jer34:10) · [EZK 16:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:8)

Associate

**Collocation:** בוא בִּבְרִית

to take (someone) into a covenant literally: to being (someone) into a covenant; hence: \= action by which humans make a solemn agreement with other humans

[1SA 20:8](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:8)

Associate

**Collocation:** בוא בְּמָסֹרֶת הַבְּרִית

to take (someone) into the bond of a covenant literally: to being (someone) into the bond of a covenant; hence: \= action by which deities make a solemn agreement with humans

[EZK 20:37](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:37)

Associate

to intermarry literally: to come among (a nation); hence: \= action by which people from one group marry people from another group

[JOS 23:12](https://ref.ly/Josh23:12) · [1KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:2) · [1KI 11:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:2)

Bear

**Collocation:** בוא בְּדָמִים

to become guilty of bloodshed literally: to come in bloodshed; hence: \= process by which humans become guilty of killing other humans

[1SA 25:26](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:26) · [1SA 25:33](https://ref.ly/1Sam25:33)

Bear

to bring (upon someone else) literally: to bring (sin or guilt upon someone else); hence: \= causative action by which entire groups are made to bear the responsibility for one individual's action

[GEN 20:9](https://ref.ly/Gen20:9) · [GEN 26:10](https://ref.ly/Gen26:10) · [EXO 32:21](https://ref.ly/Exod32:21)

Bear

to come (upon someone) literally: (one's deeds) come (upon someone); hence: \= process by which humans are held responsible for their actions and will eventually be punished

[JDG 9:24](https://ref.ly/Judg9:24)

Bear

to bring (a case) literally: to bring (a case) to (a judge); hence: \= causative action by which humans confer a case to someone who has the authority to provide justice

[EXO 18:19](https://ref.ly/Exod18:19) · [EXO 18:22](https://ref.ly/Exod18:22) · [EXO 18:26](https://ref.ly/Exod18:26)

Bear

**Collocation:** בוא עַל־יָדַיִם

to entrust, to commit to someone's charge literally: to bring (someone) on (someone else's hands); hence: \= causative action by which people in authority make other people responsible for someone else

[2KI 10:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs10:24)

Conflict

**Collocation:** בוא בְּמִשְׁפָּט

to put on trial literally: to bring (someone) into judgment; hence: \= causative action by which deities initiate a court case against humans

[JOB 14:3](https://ref.ly/Job14:3) · [ECC 11:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl11:9) · [ECC 12:14](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:14)

Control

**Collocation:** בוא בַּמְּצוּדָה

to trap in a net literally: to bring into a (trammel) net; hence: \= causative action by which deities bring animals into a trammel net, which is a metaphor for humans that are brought into a situation from which they cannot escape by themselves

[PSA 66:11](https://ref.ly/Ps66:11)

End

**Collocation:** בוא עַד־קֵץ

to come to (one's) end literally: to come to (one's) end; hence: \= process by which humans have reached the end of a certain stage in their lives

[DAN 11:45](https://ref.ly/Dan11:45)

End

**Collocation:** בוא עַד־שְׁנֹות

to come to the end of (one's) years literally: to come to (one's) years; hence: \= process by which humans have reached the end of a certain stage in their lives

[EZK 22:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek22:4)

Engage

to come upon literally: (good or evil) comes upon someone; hence: \= process by which deities humans experience good or bad in return for their actions

Happen

to bring, to bring about, to cause to happen literally: to bring (an event); hence: \= causative process by which deities cause events to take place

[GEN 6:17](https://ref.ly/Gen6:17) · [LEV 26:36](https://ref.ly/Lev26:36) · [1KI 21:29](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:29) · [2KI 19:25](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:25) · [1CH 4:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:10) · [ISA 31:2](https://ref.ly/Isa31:2) · [ISA 37:26](https://ref.ly/Isa37:26) · [ISA 46:11](https://ref.ly/Isa46:11) · [JER 4:6](https://ref.ly/Jer4:6) · [JER 40:3](https://ref.ly/Jer40:3) · [JER 49:32](https://ref.ly/Jer49:32) · [LAM 1:21](https://ref.ly/Lam1:21) · [DAN 9:24](https://ref.ly/Dan9:24)

Impact

**Collocation:** בוא בְּכִלְיָה

to shoot (arrows) into (someone's) kidney literally: to bring (arrows) into (someone's) kidney; hence: \= causative action by which deities shoot arrows into someone's kidney, which is a metaphor for inflicting deep inner suffering

[LAM 3:13](https://ref.ly/Lam3:13)

Innocent

**Collocation:** בוא בִּצְדָקָה

to be acquitted literally: to enter (God's) righteousness; hence: \= process by which human behavior is restored to conform to the requirements of the divine law

[PSA 69:28](https://ref.ly/Ps69:28)

Know

to come to, to reach, to arrive literally: (information) come to (someone); hence: \= process by which humans or deities receive information about a certain event, either directly or indirectly

Know

**Collocation:** בוא בְּשֵׁם

to be listed by name, to be mentioned by name literally: to come with a name; hence: \= state in which the names of certain people are are listed specifically as opposed to others

[1CH 4:38](https://ref.ly/1Chr4:38)25

Know

to bring literally: to bring (information) to (someone); hence: \= causative process by which humans cause (other) humans or deities to receive information about a certain event, either directly or indirectly

[GEN 37:2](https://ref.ly/Gen37:2) · [1CH 21:2](https://ref.ly/1Chr21:2) · [JOB 34:28](https://ref.ly/Job34:28)

Know

**Collocation:** בוא עֵצָה

to bring counsel, to give advice literally: to bring counsel; hence: \= causative action by which humans offer guidance and recommendations to (other) humans to help them take the right decisions

[ISA 16:3](https://ref.ly/Isa16:3)

Old

**Collocation:** בוא בַּיָּמִים

to be old literally: to come in days; hence: \= state in which humans are advanced in age

[GEN 18:11](https://ref.ly/Gen18:11) · [GEN 24:1](https://ref.ly/Gen24:1) · [JOS 13:1](https://ref.ly/Josh13:1) · [JOS 13:1](https://ref.ly/Josh13:1) · [JOS 23:1](https://ref.ly/Josh23:1) · [JOS 23:2](https://ref.ly/Josh23:2) · [1KI 1:1](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:1)

Old

**Collocation:** בוא בַּאֲנָשִׁים

to be old literally: to come among men, but probably to be read as: to come in years; hence: \= state in which humans are advanced in age

[1SA 17:12](https://ref.ly/1Sam17:12)27

Possess

**Collocation:** בוא בְּיָד

to have at hand literally: to come in (one's) hand; hence: \= state in which humans are in possession of certain objects and these objects are close at hand

[GEN 32:14](https://ref.ly/Gen32:14)

Possess

to come (to someone), to receive literally: (a possession) come to (someone); hence: \= process by which humans receive an object in possession

Relief

**Collocation:** בוא שָׁלֹום

to enter into peace literally: to enter peace; hence: \= process by which humans are freed from grief, anxiety, worries, and fear

[ISA 57:2](https://ref.ly/Isa57:2)

Safe

to bring, to lead, to guide literally: to bring; hence: \= causative process by which deities lead humans through life until they reach a certain milestone

[2SA 7:18](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:18) · [1CH 17:16](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:16) · [ECC 3:22](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:22)

Sex

to have sex, to have intercourse literally: to enter (a woman's room); hence: \= action by which a man has sexual intercourse with a woman

[GEN 6:4](https://ref.ly/Gen6:4) · [GEN 16:2](https://ref.ly/Gen16:2) · [GEN 16:4](https://ref.ly/Gen16:4) · [GEN 19:31](https://ref.ly/Gen19:31) · [GEN 29:21](https://ref.ly/Gen29:21) · [GEN 29:23](https://ref.ly/Gen29:23) · [GEN 29:30](https://ref.ly/Gen29:30) · [GEN 30:3](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3) · [GEN 30:4](https://ref.ly/Gen30:4) · [GEN 30:16](https://ref.ly/Gen30:16) · [GEN 38:2](https://ref.ly/Gen38:2) · [GEN 38:8](https://ref.ly/Gen38:8) · [GEN 38:9](https://ref.ly/Gen38:9) · [GEN 38:16](https://ref.ly/Gen38:16) · [GEN 38:16](https://ref.ly/Gen38:16) · [GEN 38:18](https://ref.ly/Gen38:18) · [DEU 21:13](https://ref.ly/Deut21:13) · [DEU 22:13](https://ref.ly/Deut22:13) · [DEU 25:5](https://ref.ly/Deut25:5) · [JDG 15:1](https://ref.ly/Judg15:1) · [JDG 16:1](https://ref.ly/Judg16:1) · [RUT 4:13](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:13) · [2SA 3:7](https://ref.ly/2Sam3:7) · [2SA 12:24](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:24) · [2SA 16:21](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:21) · [2SA 16:22](https://ref.ly/2Sam16:22) · [2SA 20:3](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:3) · [1CH 2:21](https://ref.ly/1Chr2:21) · [1CH 7:23](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:23) · [PSA 51:2](https://ref.ly/Ps51:2) · [PRO 6:29](https://ref.ly/Prov6:29) · [EZK 16:33](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:33) · [EZK 23:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:17) · [EZK 23:44](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:44) · [EZK 23:44](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:44) · [EZK 23:44](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:44)

Similar

to attain literally: to come (to a certain level); hence: \= state in which humans are equal or comparable to others with regard to a certain ability

[2SA 23:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:19) · [2SA 23:23](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:23)

Solid

to withstand fire literally: to come in the fire; hence: \= state in which an object is unaffected by fire, in that it does not burst, break, or melt

[NUM 31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:23) · [NUM 31:23](https://ref.ly/Num31:23)

Space

to place, to put literally: to bring (an object to a certain location); hence: \= causative action by which humans place an object in a certain space

[EXO 25:14](https://ref.ly/Exod25:14) · [EXO 26:11](https://ref.ly/Exod26:11) · [EXO 26:33](https://ref.ly/Exod26:33) · [EXO 27:7](https://ref.ly/Exod27:7) · [EXO 37:5](https://ref.ly/Exod37:5) · [LEV 11:32](https://ref.ly/Lev11:32)

Stage

**Collocation:** בוא עֲדִי עֲדָיִים

to reach full womanhood literally: to come to the youth of youths; hence, possibly: \= process by which a young woman reaches the time when her body becomes mature

[EZK 16:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:7)

Stance

to place, to put literally: to bring (part of one's body into a certain location); hence: \= action by which humans place part of their body in a certain location

[EXO 4:6](https://ref.ly/Exod4:6) · [EXO 4:6](https://ref.ly/Exod4:6) · [JER 27:11](https://ref.ly/Jer27:11) · [JER 27:12](https://ref.ly/Jer27:12)

Stance

**Collocation:** בוא לְמָעְלָה

to lift up, to wield literally: to bring (an object) upward; hence: \= action by which humans lift up an object that they carry in their hand

[PSA 74:5](https://ref.ly/Ps74:5)28

Think

**Collocation:** בוא לֵב

to apply (one's) mind literally: to bring (one's) heart (to a matter); hence: \= action by which humans take a matter into consideration

[PRO 23:12](https://ref.ly/Prov23:12)

Think

**Collocation:** בוא עַל־לֵב

to determine literally: to come to heart; hence: \= process by which humans come to an understanding on how they are going to achieve a particular purpose

[2CH 7:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:11)

Think

**Collocation:** בוא לְפָנִים

to determine, to decide literally: to come before (someone's) face; hence: \= process by which deities come to an conclusion as to how they are going to achieve a particular purpose

[GEN 6:13](https://ref.ly/Gen6:13)

Vow

**Collocation:** בוא בְּאָלָה

to join in taking an oath literally: to enter an oath; hence: \= action by which a group of people take a joint oath

[NEH 10:30](https://ref.ly/Neh10:30)

Vow

**Collocation:** בוא בְּאָלָה

to put under oath literally: to bring (someone) into an oath; hence: \= causative action by which humans cause other humans to take a joint oath

[EZK 17:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek17:13)

Wise

**Collocation:** בוא לְבַב חָכְמָה

to obtain a wise heart text unsure; literally: to bring a heart of wisdom; hence: \= process by which humans obtain the ability to think wisely

[PSA 90:12](https://ref.ly/Ps90:12)29

noun f

Buildings

ledge, offset \= a narrow horizontal surface projecting from the side of a building, ► used as a support for side chambers

[EZK 41:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek41:6)

name

Names of Locations

**Collocation:** לְבֹא חֲמָת

Lebo\-Hamath literally: entrace to Hamath; hence, probably: \= town, ◄ located in Syria, ► marking the northern boundary of the land promised to Israel

## בוז (id: 000868000000000)

בוז
===

בוז (H0936)

verb · בּוּז · בּוּזָה

Despise · Revile

to despise, to scorn \= state in which someone considers someone or something else unworthy of attention or respect, and shows that by his/her actions

[2KI 19:21](https://ref.ly/2Kgs19:21) · [PRO 1:7](https://ref.ly/Prov1:7) · [PRO 6:30](https://ref.ly/Prov6:30) · [PRO 11:12](https://ref.ly/Prov11:12) · [PRO 13:13](https://ref.ly/Prov13:13) · [PRO 14:21](https://ref.ly/Prov14:21) · [PRO 23:9](https://ref.ly/Prov23:9) · [PRO 23:22](https://ref.ly/Prov23:22) · [PRO 30:17](https://ref.ly/Prov30:17) · [SNG 8:1](https://ref.ly/Song8:1) · [SNG 8:7](https://ref.ly/Song8:7) · [SNG 8:7](https://ref.ly/Song8:7) · [ISA 37:22](https://ref.ly/Isa37:22) · [ZEC 4:10](https://ref.ly/Zech4:10)

Enio R. Mueller

## בּוּל (id: 000874000000000)

בּוּל
=====

בּוּל (H0944 · H0945)

noun m · יבל

Products

produce of exact meaning discussed; \= a portion of some part of nature, which is understood as having produced it; the final shape is instrumental for people and animals in some contexts

[JOB 40:20](https://ref.ly/Job40:20)1 · [ISA 44:19](https://ref.ly/Isa44:19)

name

Names of Times

Bul \= a month in pre\-exilic times, equivalent to period October/November

[1KI 6:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs6:38)

1 JOB 40:20 English versions usually interpret בוּל here as meaning "food" \| explicitly RSV NRSV "food" \| implicitly NJPS NIV "produce" \| CEV GNT "grass" \| REB "cattle" as prey. But the specialized discussion is far more open as to alternatives \| for a good overview, see Clines, Job 38–42, p.1153\.

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Job 40:20; Isaiah 44:19; 1 Kings 6:38

## בוס (id: 000876000000000)

בוס
===

בוס (H0947)

verb · מְבוּסָה · תְּבוּסָה

Stance

to kick about, to flail about \= action by which humans move their arms and legs in an uncontrolled, haphazard fashion, like a young baby

[EZK 16:6](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:6) · [EZK 16:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek16:22)

Impact

to tread down, to trample \= action by which humans tread heavily and noisily on somebody or something; ► usually with the purpose of killing, destroying, desecrating, and/or humiliating

[PSA 44:6](https://ref.ly/Ps44:6) · [PSA 60:14](https://ref.ly/Ps60:14) · [PSA 108:14](https://ref.ly/Ps108:14) · [ISA 14:19](https://ref.ly/Isa14:19) · [ISA 14:25](https://ref.ly/Isa14:25) · [ISA 41:25](https://ref.ly/Isa41:25)1 · [ISA 63:6](https://ref.ly/Isa63:6) · [ISA 63:18](https://ref.ly/Isa63:18) · [JER 12:10](https://ref.ly/Jer12:10) · [AMO 5:11](https://ref.ly/Amos5:11)2 · [ZEC 10:5](https://ref.ly/Zech10:5)3

Despise

to spurn, to loathe, to refuse \= state in which humans regard something with distaste

[PRO 27:7](https://ref.ly/Prov27:7)4

1 ISA 41:25 RSV NRSV ESV "he shall trample" NIV "he treads" NJPS "he has trampled" NJB GNT CEV "he tramples" REB "he marches over" \| MT וְיָבֹא LXX "ἐρχέσθωσαν ἄρχοντες" 2 AMO 5:11 LXX "κατακονδυλίζω" \| RSV NIV NJB NRSV ESV "trample" GNT "oppress" CEV "abuse" \| NJPS "impose a tax" REB "levy taxes" \= בשׁס 3 ZEC 10:5 Apparently the direct object has been ellided in this verse, as RSV NRSV GNT CEV insert the word "enemies" into the translation. Other translations take the phrase "dirt of the street" as the direct object, which is somewhat problematic because of the preposition בְּ. 4 PRO 27:7 Most commentaries stick to a physical sense: "tramples". BHS suggests emending to תָּבוּז "despises". Most modern versions translate it in that psychological sense, either accepting the emendation or understanding תָּבוּס in an extended sense.

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Ezekiel 16:6; Ezekiel 16:22; Psalms 44:6; Psalms 60:14; Psalms 108:14; Isaiah 14:19; Isaiah 14:25; Isaiah 41:25; Isaiah 63:6; Isaiah 63:18; Jeremiah 12:10; Amos 5:11; Zechariah 10:5; Proverbs 27:7

## בֹּור (id: 000882000000000)

בֹּור
=====

בֹּור (H0877 · H0953) — בֹּאר

noun m · באר · בְּאֵר · בַּיִר

Wells and Cisterns

pit, cistern \= hole in the ground, big enough to hold a person and deep enough that a person cannot climb out; ◄ usually dug out of the ground or hewn from rock; ► often used to collect and store rain water; ≈ fresh water from a spring is considered superior to stagnant water from a cistern

10:6](https://ref.ly/Hos10:6) · [JOL 1:11](https://ref.ly/Joel1:11) · [JOL 2:26](https://ref.ly/Joel2:26) · [JOL 2:27](https://ref.ly/Joel2:27) · [MIC 3:7](https://ref.ly/Mic3:7) · [MIC 7:16](https://ref.ly/Mic7:16) · [ZEP 3:11](https://ref.ly/Zeph3:11) · [ZEC 13:4](https://ref.ly/Zech13:4)

Shame

to be ashamed \= state in which someone has forfeited the respect of the community, due to a situation that is not in accordance to someone's status

[GEN 2:25](https://ref.ly/Gen2:25)

Shame

to put to shame, to bring shame \= causative action by which someone causes someone else to forfeit the respect of the community, by provoking a situation that is not in accordance to that person's status

[2SA 19:6](https://ref.ly/2Sam19:6) · [PSA 14:6](https://ref.ly/Ps14:6) · [PSA 44:8](https://ref.ly/Ps44:8) · [PSA 53:6](https://ref.ly/Ps53:6) · [PSA 119:31](https://ref.ly/Ps119:31) · [PSA 119:116](https://ref.ly/Ps119:116) · [PRO 10:5](https://ref.ly/Prov10:5) · [PRO 12:4](https://ref.ly/Prov12:4) · [PRO 14:35](https://ref.ly/Prov14:35) · [PRO 17:2](https://ref.ly/Prov17:2) · [PRO 19:26](https://ref.ly/Prov19:26) · [PRO 29:15](https://ref.ly/Prov29:15) · [HOS 2:7](https://ref.ly/Hos2:7) · [ZEC 10:5](https://ref.ly/Zech10:5)

End

to fail, to come to naught probably a pun on יבשׁ "to dry up": \= state in which an event does not happen as expected ◄ as if it withers before coming to fruition and those who are waiting for it are disappointed

[JOL 1:12](https://ref.ly/Joel1:12) · [ZEC 9:5](https://ref.ly/Zech9:5)

verb

Dry

to dry up \= state of being without water or moisture; ≈ possibly with an implicit connotation of shame

[HOS 13:15](https://ref.ly/Hos13:15)

verb

Delay

**Collocation:** אחר

to delay, to be delayed \= action by which someone takes much more time than expected to do something; ≈ possibly with an implicit connotation of bringing some sort of disappointment to those expecting

[EXO 32:1](https://ref.ly/Exod32:1) · [JDG 5:28](https://ref.ly/Judg5:28)

1 ISA 30:5 RSV NRSV ESV "comes to shame" NIV "be put to shame" NJPS "come to shame" NJB "be disappointed" \| MT הֹבִאישׁ \= באשׁ

Enio R. Mueller

1 1SA 11:8 Some distinguish this town from the other, identifying it as Khirbet Ibzek, north of Shechem, and the one mentioned in JDG 1:4,5 as Khirbet Bezka, north of Jerusalem. But it is probable that there was only one town with this name, to be identified with Khirbet Bezka (Schearing, NIDB 1: 452\).

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Judges 1:4; Judges 1:5; 1 Samuel 11:8

## בחן (id: 000907000000000)

בחן
===

בחן (H0974)

verb · בֹּחַן

Test

to test \= action by which humans or deities examine the character of other humans or deities by having them engage in a certain activity, ◄ as a goldsmith tests the quality of a precious metal by smelting it in the fire

[GEN 42:15](https://ref.ly/Gen42:15) · [1CH 29:17](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:17) · [JOB 7:18](https://ref.ly/Job7:18) · [JOB 34:36](https://ref.ly/Job34:36) · [PSA 7:10](https://ref.ly/Ps7:10) · [PSA 11:4](https://ref.ly/Ps11:4) · [PSA 11:5](https://ref.ly/Ps11:5) · [PSA 17:3](https://ref.ly/Ps17:3) · [PSA 26:2](https://ref.ly/Ps26:2) · [PSA 81:8](https://ref.ly/Ps81:8) · [PSA 95:9](https://ref.ly/Ps95:9) · [PSA 139:23](https://ref.ly/Ps139:23) · [PRO 17:3](https://ref.ly/Prov17:3) · [JER 6:27](https://ref.ly/Jer6:27) · [JER 11:20](https://ref.ly/Jer11:20) · [JER 12:3](https://ref.ly/Jer12:3) · [JER 17:10](https://ref.ly/Jer17:10) · [JER 20:12](https://ref.ly/Jer20:12) · [EZK 21:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:18)1 · [MAL 3:10](https://ref.ly/Mal3:10) · [MAL 3:15](https://ref.ly/Mal3:15)

Test · Good

to refine \= causative action by which a goldsmith improves the quality of a precious metal by smelting it in the fire and so removing all contaminants; ≈ often used as a metaphor for purging evil from the hearts of humans

[ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9)

Test · Just

to refine \= causative action by which deities improve the character of humans by leading them through difficult circumstances, ◄ like a goldsmith improves the quality of a precious metal by smelting it in the fire and so removing all contaminants

[JOB 23:10](https://ref.ly/Job23:10) · [PSA 66:10](https://ref.ly/Ps66:10) · [JER 9:6](https://ref.ly/Jer9:6) · [ZEC 13:9](https://ref.ly/Zech13:9)

Test · True

to test \= action by which humans try to determine whether the words spoken by others are true and reliable, ◄ as a goldsmith tests the quality of a precious metal by smelting it in the fire

[GEN 42:16](https://ref.ly/Gen42:16) · [JOB 12:11](https://ref.ly/Job12:11) · [JOB 34:3](https://ref.ly/Job34:3)

1 EZK 21:18 NRSV NJPS "consider" \= \[1b] \| RSV NIV "testing" REB "test" GNT CEV "I am testing" \= בֹּחַן

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 42:15; 1 Chronicles 29:17; Job 7:18; Job 34:36; Psalms 7:10; Psalms 11:4; Psalms 11:5; Psalms 17:3; Psalms 26:2; Psalms 81:8; Psalms 95:9; Psalms 139:23; Proverbs 17:3; Jeremiah 6:27; Jeremiah 11:20; Jeremiah 12:3; Jeremiah 17:10; Jeremiah 20:12; Ezekiel 21:18; Malachi 3:10; Malachi 3:15; Zechariah 13:9; Job 23:10; Psalms 66:10; Jeremiah 9:6; Genesis 42:16; Job 12:11; Job 34:3

Good

choice, picked, to be preferred \= state in which an object has been selected for its superior quality

[EXO 14:7](https://ref.ly/Exod14:7) · [PRO 8:10](https://ref.ly/Prov8:10) · [PRO 8:19](https://ref.ly/Prov8:19) · [PRO 10:20](https://ref.ly/Prov10:20) · [PRO 16:16](https://ref.ly/Prov16:16) · [SNG 5:15](https://ref.ly/Song5:15)

Well

[PRO 21:3](https://ref.ly/Prov21:3) · [PRO 22:1](https://ref.ly/Prov22:1)

Soldiers

warrior, soldier \= person selected from among the young men of a tribe or nation to join the army

[JDG 20:15](https://ref.ly/Judg20:15) · [JDG 20:16](https://ref.ly/Judg20:16) · [JDG 20:34](https://ref.ly/Judg20:34) · [1SA 24:3](https://ref.ly/1Sam24:3) · [1SA 26:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam26:2) · [2SA 6:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam6:1) · [2SA 10:9](https://ref.ly/2Sam10:9) · [1KI 12:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs12:21) · [1CH 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr19:10) · [2CH 11:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr11:1) · [2CH 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:3) · [2CH 13:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:3) · [2CH 13:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:17) · [2CH 25:5](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:5)

verb

Know

to test, to examine \= action by which someone evaluates the dependability and/or reliability of someone else ◄ as an assayer examines ore or metal

[JOB 34:4](https://ref.ly/Job34:4)1 · [JOB 36:21](https://ref.ly/Job36:21)2 · [ISA 48:10](https://ref.ly/Isa48:10)4

1 JOB 34:33 · JOB 34:4 RSV NRSV "choose" NJPS GNT CEV "decide" \= \[1] \| NIV "discern" NJB "consider" REB "examine" \= \[2] 2 JOB 36:21 RSV Gordis "choose" NIV "prefer" \= \[1] \| NJPS NRSV REB "to try" \= pual form בֻּחַרְתָּ \= \[2] 3 ECC 9:4 MT\-K יבּחר has been variously read (יְבֻחַר pual \| יִבָּחֶר niphal) \| MT\-Q יְחֻבַּר from חבר "to join". Most English versions follow MT\-Q (RSV NRSV "is joined with" \| NJPS "is reckoned among" \| NIV "is among" \| REB "is counted among") as also most commentaries. Seouw takes the MT\-K as qal, and reads the phrase as interrogative (rhetorical question) "Who is the one who chooses?". Murphy also takes it as interrogative, "who can be set apart?" (understanding בחר here as "to be set apart"). The existence of a pual form has been questioned (cf. Seouw, and Gordis, The Book of Job, 418, on JOB 36:21\). 4 ISA 48:10 NJB "chosen" \= \[1] \| RSV "tried" NIV NJPS NRSV GNT CEV "tested" REB "purified" \= \[2]

Enio R. Mueller

==========

בַּטֻּחֹות (H0987) — בַּטֻּחֹות

noun f · בטח

Confident

confidence, security \= state in which humans live with confidence and without fear; ≈ often regarded as carelessness, especially when there is no mention of trust in YHWH

[JOB 12:6](https://ref.ly/Job12:6)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Job 12:6


## בטל (id: 000919000000000)

בטל
===

בטל (H0988 · A0989)

verb

Non\-Engage

to cease working, to become idle \= state in which someone is no longer engaged in an action one used to be engaged in previously

[EZR 4:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:24) · [EZR 4:24](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:24) · [ECC 12:3](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:3)1

Non\-Engage

to cause (someone or some activity) to stop \= causative action by which humans cause other humans no longer to be engaged in an action that they were engaged in previously

[EZR 4:21](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:21) · [EZR 4:23](https://ref.ly/Ezra4:23) · [EZR 5:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:5) · [EZR 6:8](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:8)

1 ECC 12:3 The referent of בטל here is discussed. RSV NIV "grinders" \| NRSV NJPS REB "women/maids that grind", all have a sociological referent. CEV GNT understand it as a metaphor (grinders \> teeth) for an aged human body

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Ezra 4:24; Ecclesiastes 12:3; Ezra 4:21; Ezra 4:23; Ezra 5:5; Ezra 6:8

בֶּטֶן

בֶּטֶן (H0990 · H0991)

noun f

belly, womb, stomach, inmost parts \= front part of the human trunk below the ribs, containing the stomach, bowels, and reproductive organs; ≈ associated with birth, food, speech, and the innermost part of objects

People

## בָּטְנִים (id: 000922000000000)

בָּטְנִים
=========

בָּטְנִים (H0992) — בָּטְנָה

noun m

Fruits

pistachio nuts \= nuts with a hard, red, heart\-shaped shell of about 2 cm long, with an oily green kernel inside; comes from a small tree with many branches that lose their leaves in winter, Pistacia vera, of the cashew family; ≈ regarded as valuable

[GEN 43:11](https://ref.ly/Gen43:11)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Genesis 43:11

בין

בין (H0995)

verb · בַּיִן · בֵּנַיִם · בִּינָה · תְּבוּנָה · יָבִין

Wise

Know

to look closely, to take note, to pay attention, to consider, to understand, to draw understanding \= action by which someone observes something carefully, with the intention of seeing through the outward appearance of things and grasping their essence, and so gain knowledge and understanding

Know

to instruct, to give instruction, to teach, to give understanding, to explain \= pattern of actions by which someone helps others to gain knowledge and understanding by observing carefully in order to see through the outward appearance of things and grasp their essence

## בִּירָה (id: 000929000000000)

בִּירָה
=======

בִּירָה (A1001 · H1002 · H1003) — בִּירָנִית, בִּירָנִיָּה

noun f

Buildings

fortress, citadel \= a fortress that commands a city and is used in the control of the inhabitants and in defense during attack or siege.

[2CH 17:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr17:12) · [2CH 27:4](https://ref.ly/2Chr27:4) · [EZR 6:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:2) · [NEH 1:1](https://ref.ly/Neh1:1) · [NEH 7:2](https://ref.ly/Neh7:2) · [EST 1:2](https://ref.ly/Esth1:2) · [EST 1:5](https://ref.ly/Esth1:5) · [EST 2:3](https://ref.ly/Esth2:3) · [EST 2:5](https://ref.ly/Esth2:5) · [EST 2:8](https://ref.ly/Esth2:8) · [EST 3:15](https://ref.ly/Esth3:15) · [EST 8:14](https://ref.ly/Esth8:14) · [EST 9:6](https://ref.ly/Esth9:6) · [EST 9:11](https://ref.ly/Esth9:11) · [EST 9:12](https://ref.ly/Esth9:12) · [DAN 8:2](https://ref.ly/Dan8:2)

Buildings

temple (of Jerusalem) \= a complex of buildings in Jerusalem, including the temple area

[1CH 29:1](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:1)1 · [1CH 29:19](https://ref.ly/1Chr29:19)1 · [NEH 2:8](https://ref.ly/Neh2:8)1

1 1CH 29:1 · 1CH 29:19 · NEH 2:8 The meaning of בִּירָה here is disputed. Since it is a building or a series of buildings לַיהוָה אֶלֹהִים the referent must be the temple or the temple area. RSV "palace" NIV "palatial structure" \| NRSV NJPS CEV GNT "temple". NRSV has "temple" in the text and a note "Heb. fortress". Klein has "temple" \| Braun "palace" (but with the sense of "temple", cf. note). Knoppers 944 has "citadel" and explains: "there is reason to believe that bîrâ refers here to a complex of buildings, including the Temple (J. Schwartz 1996: 29–49\), rather than to a single structure".

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** 2 Chronicles 17:12; 2 Chronicles 27:4; Ezra 6:2; Nehemiah 1:1; Nehemiah 7:2; Esther 1:2; Esther 1:5; Esther 2:3; Esther 2:5; Esther 2:8; Esther 3:15; Esther 8:14; Esther 9:6; Esther 9:11; Esther 9:12; Daniel 8:2; 1 Chronicles 29:1; 1 Chronicles 29:19; Nehemiah 2:8

Space

place literally: house (of an object); hence: \= a place that is associated with a certain class people that live there, or objects that are found there

[EXO 13:3](https://ref.ly/Exod13:3) · [EXO 13:14](https://ref.ly/Exod13:14) · [EXO 20:2](https://ref.ly/Exod20:2) · [DEU 5:6](https://ref.ly/Deut5:6) · [DEU 6:12](https://ref.ly/Deut6:12) · [DEU 7:8](https://ref.ly/Deut7:8) · [DEU 8:14](https://ref.ly/Deut8:14) · [DEU 13:6](https://ref.ly/Deut13:6) · [DEU 13:11](https://ref.ly/Deut13:11) · [JOS 24:17](https://ref.ly/Josh24:17) · [JDG 6:8](https://ref.ly/Judg6:8) · [NEH 2:3](https://ref.ly/Neh2:3) · [PRO 8:2](https://ref.ly/Prov8:2) · [JER 34:13](https://ref.ly/Jer34:13) · [MIC 6:4](https://ref.ly/Mic6:4)

Containers

holder, container literally: house (of an object); hence: \= an object that serves as a holder or container of another object, ◄ as if it were its house

[EXO 25:27](https://ref.ly/Exod25:27) · [EXO 26:29](https://ref.ly/Exod26:29) · [EXO 30:4](https://ref.ly/Exod30:4) · [EXO 36:34](https://ref.ly/Exod36:34) · [EXO 37:14](https://ref.ly/Exod37:14) · [EXO 37:27](https://ref.ly/Exod37:27) · [EXO 38:5](https://ref.ly/Exod38:5) · [1KI 18:32](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:32) · [ISA 3:20](https://ref.ly/Isa3:20)

Groups

household, family, descendants, dynasty literally: house; hence: \= a group of people associated with a house, such as the family living there, or the descendants of the head of the household

inside \= relation linking a proposition to a location: the part of an object that is visible from the perspective of someone located within that object

Weak

**Collocation:** שׁכן בָּתֵּי חֹמֶר

to dwell in houses of clay \> to be a mortal human being literally: to dwell in houses of clay; hence: \= state of being a mortal human being, living in a body that is bound to return to dust one day

[JOB 4:19](https://ref.ly/Job4:19)

1 JDG 17:12 RSV NIV NJB NRSV CEV ESV "house" NJPS "shrine" REB "household" GNT "home" 2 2KI 16:18 RSV NJPS NRSV "palace" NIV "temple" \= \[a] \| NJB "inside" \= \[e] 3 2CH 34:6 MT\-K בהר בתיהם REB "he burnt down their houses" \| MT\-Q בְּחַרְבֹתֵיהֶם RSV NRSV ESV "in their ruins" NIV "in the ruins" NJPS "in ruins" NJB "their open spaces" 4 ISA 23:1 MT HOTTP\-C RSV NIV NJPS NJB GNT CEV ESV "house(s)" NJB "fortress" \| REB מָבִית \= "harbor" 5 JER 52:11 MT\-K בבית \| MT\-Q בֵית 6 ZEP 1:9 RSV NRSV GNT ESV "(master's) house" NIV NJB "temple" NJPS "palace" REB "(Lord's) house" 7 JOB 27:18 MT HOTTP\-B NIV CEV "cocoon" ESV "house" \| RSV NJB GNT "web" \| NJPS NRSV REB "nest" 8 2SA 21:1 Most modern versions understand this expression here as a straight reference to the household of Saul, what could have support in v.4\. 9 PSA 68:13 The exact meaning of בַּיִת here is discussed \| it depends much on which meaning is attributed to נְוַת, usually seen as referring to women. NIV "in the camps men..." \| Dahood (Psalms II: 51–100\) "the country's pasture land".

## בֵּית אָוֶן (id: 000932000000000)

בֵּית אָוֶן
===========

בֵּית אָוֶן (H1007)

name · בַּיִת · אָוֶן

Names of Locations

Beth\-Aven \= town; ◄ territory of Benjamin

[JOS 7:2](https://ref.ly/Josh7:2) · [JOS 18:12](https://ref.ly/Josh18:12) · [1SA 13:5](https://ref.ly/1Sam13:5) · [1SA 14:23](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:23) · [HOS 5:8](https://ref.ly/Hos5:8)

Names of Locations

Beth\-Aven \= town; ◄ territory of Ephraim ≈ probably a pun or word\-play on the name Bethel ("house of God")

[HOS 4:15](https://ref.ly/Hos4:15) · [HOS 10:5](https://ref.ly/Hos10:5)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Joshua 7:2; Joshua 18:12; 1 Samuel 13:5; 1 Samuel 14:23; Hosea 5:8; Hosea 4:15; Hosea 10:5

## בלה (id: 001010000000000)

בלה
===

בלה (A1080 · H1086) — בלא

verb · בַּל · בָּלֶה · בְּלֹוי · בְּלִי · בְּלִיַּעַל · בִּלְתִּי · תַּבְלִית

Impact

to wear out, to become worn out, to waste away \= process by which objects lose their quality and strength due to continued use; ≈ used figuratively to denote the loss of a person's vitality

[GEN 18:12](https://ref.ly/Gen18:12) · [DEU 8:4](https://ref.ly/Deut8:4) · [DEU 29:4](https://ref.ly/Deut29:4) · [DEU 29:4](https://ref.ly/Deut29:4) · [JOS 9:13](https://ref.ly/Josh9:13) · [NEH 9:21](https://ref.ly/Neh9:21) · [PSA 32:3](https://ref.ly/Ps32:3)

Non\-Exist

to wear out, to waste away \= to gradually disappear from existence ◄ like a cloth that slowly wears out and finally falls apart

[JOB 13:28](https://ref.ly/Job13:28) · [PSA 102:27](https://ref.ly/Ps102:27) · [ISA 50:9](https://ref.ly/Isa50:9) · [ISA 51:6](https://ref.ly/Isa51:6) · [ISA 64:5](https://ref.ly/Isa64:5)

Impact

to cause to waste away, to use up \= action by which someone causes objects to gradually lose their quality and strength

[1CH 17:9](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:9) · [PSA 49:15](https://ref.ly/Ps49:15) · [ISA 65:22](https://ref.ly/Isa65:22) · [LAM 3:4](https://ref.ly/Lam3:4) · [DAN 7:25](https://ref.ly/Dan7:25)

1 JOB 21:13 MT\-K יבלו \| MT\-Q יְכַלּוּ \= כלל

Mixed

to mix (oneself) with (other people) \= action by which people mix with other groups of people and adapt their ways of thinking and practices

[HOS 7:8](https://ref.ly/Hos7:8)2

Rub

to be anointed \= process by which fresh oil is poured over someone's head; ◄ possibly in analogy to the oil that was poured over the grain offering; ► possibly in order to distinguish this person with the honor of a special calling

[PSA 92:11](https://ref.ly/Ps92:11)3

Change

**Collocation:** בלה שָׂפָה

to confuse literally: to mix language; hence: \= action by which a divine being changes a language in such a way that it is no longer understood; ◄ as if it were changed from a well ordered system into an unordered mass

[GEN 11:7](https://ref.ly/Gen11:7) · [GEN 11:9](https://ref.ly/Gen11:9)

1 JDG 19:21 MT\-K ויבול \| MT\-Q וַיָּבָל 2 HOS 7:8 Here בלל could have a negative connotation, as in GEN 11:7,9\. See the note in MacIntosh, who translates it as "vitiated", a meaning that would "fit this particular context and the reference to the spoiled cake better than the other sense of the verb, viz. ‘mix’, ‘mingle’, though the two senses are clearly related". HALOT suggests the meaning "to be thrown about", without explanation (the reference to Rudolph, which does not appear in HALAT, is misleading). 3 PSA 92:11 For a discussion, see the note in Tate.

Enio R. Mueller

Ingest

[GEN 41:7](https://ref.ly/Gen41:7) · [GEN 41:24](https://ref.ly/Gen41:24) · [EXO 7:12](https://ref.ly/Exod7:12) · [EXO 15:12](https://ref.ly/Exod15:12) · [NUM 4:20](https://ref.ly/Num4:20) · [NUM 16:30](https://ref.ly/Num16:30) · [NUM 16:32](https://ref.ly/Num16:32) · [NUM 16:34](https://ref.ly/Num16:34) · [NUM 26:10](https://ref.ly/Num26:10) · [DEU 11:6](https://ref.ly/Deut11:6) · [JOB 7:19](https://ref.ly/Job7:19) · [JOB 20:15](https://ref.ly/Job20:15) · [JOB 20:18](https://ref.ly/Job20:18) · [PSA 69:16](https://ref.ly/Ps69:16) · [PSA 106:17](https://ref.ly/Ps106:17) · [PSA 124:3](https://ref.ly/Ps124:3) · [PRO 1:12](https://ref.ly/Prov1:12) · [PRO 19:28](https://ref.ly/Prov19:28) · [PRO 21:20](https://ref.ly/Prov21:20) · [ISA 28:4](https://ref.ly/Isa28:4) · [JER 51:34](https://ref.ly/Jer51:34) · [HOS 8:7](https://ref.ly/Hos8:7) · [HOS 8:8](https://ref.ly/Hos8:8) · [JON 2:1](https://ref.ly/Jonah2:1)

Non\-Exist

to swallow up, to destroy \= causative action by which deities or humans cause other humans and objects to be removed from existence in a violent way, as if by swallowing up, either by direct intervention or by accomplishing this through the hands of others

[2SA 17:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam17:16)2 · [2SA 20:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:19) · [2SA 20:20](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:20) · [JOB 2:3](https://ref.ly/Job2:3) · [JOB 8:18](https://ref.ly/Job8:18) · [JOB 10:8](https://ref.ly/Job10:8) · [JOB 37:20](https://ref.ly/Job37:20)3 · [PSA 21:10](https://ref.ly/Ps21:10) · [PSA 35:25](https://ref.ly/Ps35:25) · [PSA 55:10](https://ref.ly/Ps55:10)4 · [ECC 10:12](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:12) · [ISA 25:7](https://ref.ly/Isa25:7) · [ISA 25:8](https://ref.ly/Isa25:8) · [ISA 49:19](https://ref.ly/Isa49:19) · [LAM 2:2](https://ref.ly/Lam2:2) · [LAM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Lam2:5) · [LAM 2:5](https://ref.ly/Lam2:5) · [LAM 2:8](https://ref.ly/Lam2:8) · [LAM 2:16](https://ref.ly/Lam2:16) · [HAB 1:13](https://ref.ly/Hab1:13)

Fail

to be to no avail exact meaning unsure; possibly: \= state in which a mind event does not produce the expected outcome, as if one's senses have been destroyed

[PSA 107:27](https://ref.ly/Ps107:27)

Fail

to confuse, to confound, to frustrate exact meaning unsure; possibly: \= causative action by which someone causes a mind event not to produce the expected outcome

[PSA 55:10](https://ref.ly/Ps55:10)4 · [ISA 3:12](https://ref.ly/Isa3:12) · [ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3)

Non\-Think

to be confused exact meaning unsure; possibly: \= to be unable to think clearly, as if one's mind is failing

[JOB 37:20](https://ref.ly/Job37:20)3 · [ISA 9:15](https://ref.ly/Isa9:15) · [ISA 28:7](https://ref.ly/Isa28:7)

בַּלְעֲדֵי
==========

בַּלְעֲדֵי (H1107) — בִּלְעֲדֵי

prep

Negators

other than, apart from, without \= particle indicating that a certain state is not true or that an event does not take place without the involvement of someone else

Description

that which not meaning unsure; possibly: \= particle linking two elements, in which Y modifies the information presented by X, by which X represents an implicit entity denoting an object, location, time, or manner, and Y represents a negated proposition in which X is one of the semantic arguments

back \= rear surface of the body

inhabitants of, originating from literally: children of a town, region, nation; hence: \= people, animals, or products that are inhabitants of or originating from a place, town, region, or nation, ◄ as if they were the offspring of that place

Titles

(your) son \> (your) subordinate \= title used by a male person to address someone else whom he considers superior to himself, ◄ as if he were that person's child

[2KI 8:9](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:9) · [2KI 16:7](https://ref.ly/2Kgs16:7)

Titles

my son, my children \= title used to address a person (male if in singular) who is regarded with some degree of affection, ◄ as if he were the speaker's child

Names of People

Ben \= man; ► Levite; time of king David

noun m

Names of Constructions

**Collocation:** שַׁעַר בְּנֵי־הָעָם

the People's Gate \= one of the gates in Jerusalem, ► through which the kings of Judah used to enter and leave the city

[JER 17:19](https://ref.ly/Jer17:19)18

1 DEU 2:33 MT\-K בנו \| MT\-Q בָּנָיו 2 DEU 33:9 · 1SA 30:6 MT\-K בנו \| MT\-Q בָּנָיו 3 2KI 4:7 MT\-K בניכי \| MT\-Q וּבָנַיִךְ 4 2KI 19:37 MT\-K \| MT\-Q בָּנָיו 5 1CH 6:11 MT\-K בנו \| MT\-Q בְּנֵי 6 1CH 22:7 MT\-K בנו \| MT\-Q בְּנִי 7 1CH 24:26 NJPS NJB REB "his son" \| RSV NIV NRSV ESV "Beno" \| GNT CEV omit 8 2CH 11:18 MT\-K בן \| MT\-Q בַּת 9 PSA 73:15 RSV NIV NJB NRSV ESV "children" \| NJPS "disciples" \| REB GNT CEV "people" 10 PSA 80:16 MT HOTTP\-A NIV ESV "son" NJPS "stem" GNT "young vine" CEV "your very own" \| RSV NJB NRSV REB omit phrase 11 DAN 11:10 MT\-K ובנו \| MT\-Q וּבָנָיו 12 HOS 13:15 MT \| HOTTP\-B NIV NJPS NRSV REB ESV "among" \= בַּיִן 13 EZK 2:4 NRSV "descendants" \| RSV NIV "the people", connecting הַבָּנִים in 2:4 to בְּנֵי יִשְׁרָאֵל in 2:3\. 14 PSA 80:16 The exact meaning of בֵּן in this verse has been much discussed. Interpretation has to take into account the suggestion in BHS apparatus that the second line of this verse, which contains the word, may be a redactional addition from v.18 (where the same phrase occurs again) and should be deleted (cf. RSV NRSV REB). If it is maintained as part of the verse, then v.18 helps in getting the meaning of the word here, since there it is paralleled by אִישׁ יְמִינֶךָ "the man of Your right" and by בֶּן־אָדָם "son of man". Most modern interpreters understand that these three expressions apply to the king, the "son" of God, the one at His right hand, perhaps an archetypical "son of man" (with messianic connotations?). 15 2SA 23:20 MT\-K חי \| MT\-Q חַיִל 16 JER 48:45 RSV ESV "sons of tumult" NIV "noisy boasters" NJB "turbulent brood" NRSV "people of tumult" REB "braggarts" GNT "war\-loving" CEV "warlike" \| NJPS "people of Shaon" 17 1CH 15:18 MT NJPS \| RSV NIV NJB NRSV REB GNT CEV ESV omit 18 JER 17:19 MT\-K HOTTP\-B בני־עם MK\-Q בְּנֵי־הָעָם NIV "gate of the people" NJPS NRSV GNT ESV "People's Gate" NJB "Gate of the Sons of the People" \| RSV REB "Benjamin Gate" \= בנימן 19 PSA 9:1 MT עַלמוּת לַבֵּן \| NIV CEV "Death of the Son" \| NJPS "almuth labben" RSV NRSV REB ESV "Muth labben"

Enio R. Mueller

Happen

**Collocation:** בנה בַּיִת

to establish a family line, to establish a dynasty literally: to build a house (for someone); hence: \= action by which humans provide posterity for someone else

[DEU 25:9](https://ref.ly/Deut25:9) · [RUT 4:11](https://ref.ly/Ruth4:11) · [1SA 2:35](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:35) · [2SA 7:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam7:27) · [1KI 11:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:38) · [1KI 11:38](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:38) · [1CH 17:10](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:10) · [1CH 17:25](https://ref.ly/1Chr17:25)

Birth

to receive a family literally: to be built; hence: \= action by which a slave woman gives birth to a child on behalf of her mistress, so that the continuity of the family line is ensured

[GEN 16:2](https://ref.ly/Gen16:2) · [GEN 30:3](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3)

1 1KI 8:48 · 1KI 8:48 MT\-K בניתֵ \| MT\-Q בָּנִיתִי 2 2CH 33:16 MT\-Q \| MT\-K ויכן \= כון 3 JOB 22:23 MT HOTTP\-B NIV NJPS NRSV "you will be restored" ESV "you will be built up" CEV "all will go well for you" \| LXX "ταπεινώσῃς σεαυτὸν" RSV "humble yourself" NJB "humbled" REB "in sincerity" GNT "humbly"

## בִּנּוּי (id: 001054000000000)

בִּנּוּי
========

בִּנּוּי (H1131)

name · בנה

Names of People

Binnui \= man; ► father of Noadiah, Levite, after exile

to kick at, to despise, to treat lightly \= action by which someone shows a blatant lack of respect to something else

[1SA 2:29](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:29)1

1 1SA 2:29 NJPS "trample upon" preserves the image of the metaphor \| NIV "scorn" \| REB (CEV) "disrespect" give the meaning. RSV NRSV (GNT) "look with greed" follow LXX (supported by a Qumran manuscript) επεβλεψασ επι (hebr. תביט ). Cf. the note in Stoebe 116\.

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Deuteronomy 32:15; 1 Samuel 2:29

Burn

to burn, to consume \= action by which someone causes something else to be harmed or destroyed by fire

[EXO 22:4](https://ref.ly/Exod22:4)2 · [LEV 6:5](https://ref.ly/Lev6:5) · [JDG 15:5](https://ref.ly/Judg15:5) · [2CH 28:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr28:3) · [NEH 10:35](https://ref.ly/Neh10:35) · [PSA 83:15](https://ref.ly/Ps83:15) · [ISA 40:16](https://ref.ly/Isa40:16) · [ISA 44:15](https://ref.ly/Isa44:15) · [ISA 50:11](https://ref.ly/Isa50:11) · [EZK 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezek5:2) · [EZK 21:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:4) · [EZK 39:9](https://ref.ly/Ezek39:9) · [NAM 2:14](https://ref.ly/Nah2:14)

Burn

to light (a lamp) \= action by which someone causes a lamp to burn by setting fire to its wick

[2CH 4:20](https://ref.ly/2Chr4:20) · [2CH 13:11](https://ref.ly/2Chr13:11)

Burn

to light (a brazier or firepot) \= action by which someone causes a brazier or firepot to produce heat by setting fire to the wood in it

[JER 36:22](https://ref.ly/Jer36:22)

Happen

to burn \> to rage literally: to burn; hence: \= process by which events such as evil and anger happen with intensity and with consequences, as if it were fire

[EST 1:12](https://ref.ly/Esth1:12) · [PSA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ps2:12) · [PSA 79:5](https://ref.ly/Ps79:5) · [PSA 89:47](https://ref.ly/Ps89:47) · [ISA 9:17](https://ref.ly/Isa9:17) · [ISA 30:27](https://ref.ly/Isa30:27) · [ISA 42:25](https://ref.ly/Isa42:25) · [JER 4:4](https://ref.ly/Jer4:4) · [JER 7:20](https://ref.ly/Jer7:20) · [JER 21:12](https://ref.ly/Jer21:12) · [JER 44:6](https://ref.ly/Jer44:6) · [LAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Lam2:3) · [HOS 7:6](https://ref.ly/Hos7:6)

Non\-Exist

to sweep away, to put away, to get rid of, to do away with, to remove, to clear out, to purge, to destroy \= action by which someone makes sure that a certain object is no longer found anywhere at all, ◄ as if it were completely consumed by fire

[NUM 24:22](https://ref.ly/Num24:22) · [DEU 26:13](https://ref.ly/Deut26:13) · [DEU 26:14](https://ref.ly/Deut26:14) · [2SA 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:11) · [1KI 14:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:10) · [1KI 14:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs14:10) · [1KI 16:3](https://ref.ly/1Kgs16:3) · [1KI 21:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs21:21) · [1KI 22:47](https://ref.ly/1Kgs22:47) · [2KI 23:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs23:24) · [2CH 19:3](https://ref.ly/2Chr19:3) · [ISA 4:4](https://ref.ly/Isa4:4)

End

to sweep away, to remove, to banish, to purge \= action by which someone makes sure that a certain event no longer takes place anywhere at all

Non\-Exist

to plunder, to strip \= action by which someone removes things from a certain location with violence

בצע
===

בצע (H1214)

verb · בֶּצַע

Detach

to cut off, to break off \= causative action by which someone severs a link between one object and a larger object of which it used to be a part

[ISA 38:12](https://ref.ly/Isa38:12) · [AMO 9:1](https://ref.ly/Amos9:1)

Complete

to complete, to carry out, to finish \= a causative process by which deities or humans bring a process to its end by completing it in its entirety

[ISA 10:12](https://ref.ly/Isa10:12) · [LAM 2:17](https://ref.ly/Lam2:17) · [ZEC 4:9](https://ref.ly/Zech4:9)

Dead

to be cut off \= process by which someone's life comes to an end, as if being cut off from the community of the living

[JOB 27:8](https://ref.ly/Job27:8) · [JER 51:13](https://ref.ly/Jer51:13)

Dead

to cut off, to do away with \= causative action by which a deity ends someone's life, as if cutting that person off from the community of the living

[JOB 6:9](https://ref.ly/Job6:9)

Non\-Move

to be stopped, to be halted exact meaning unsure; possibly: \= process by which an event comes to a standstill because someone or something makes it impossible to continue

[JOL 2:8](https://ref.ly/Joel2:8)1

Wish

to pursue unjust gain \= pattern of actions by which someone makes an effort to get personal gain; ≈ with a negative connotation, of unjust gain

## בקע (id: 001141000000000)

בקע
===

בקע (H1234)

verb · בָּקִיעַ · בֶּקַע · בִּקְעָה

Non\-Whole

to be split, to be cleft, to be breached, to be cracked, to be ripped open, to be rent, to burst, to burst asunder \= process by which an object is split, either partially or completely, either by supernatural means or by violence

[EXO 14:21](https://ref.ly/Exod14:21) · [NUM 16:31](https://ref.ly/Num16:31) · [JOS 9:4](https://ref.ly/Josh9:4) · [JOS 9:13](https://ref.ly/Josh9:13) · [1KI 1:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs1:40) · [2KI 25:4](https://ref.ly/2Kgs25:4) · [2CH 25:12](https://ref.ly/2Chr25:12) · [JOB 26:8](https://ref.ly/Job26:8) · [JOB 32:19](https://ref.ly/Job32:19) · [JER 39:2](https://ref.ly/Jer39:2) · [JER 52:7](https://ref.ly/Jer52:7) · [EZK 26:10](https://ref.ly/Ezek26:10) · [EZK 30:16](https://ref.ly/Ezek30:16) · [HOS 14:1](https://ref.ly/Hos14:1) · [MIC 1:4](https://ref.ly/Mic1:4) · [ZEC 14:4](https://ref.ly/Zech14:4)

Non\-Whole

to split, to cleave, to rip open, to cut, to rend, to maul, to divide, to break through \= causative action by which deities or human cause an object to split, either partially or completely, either by supernatural means or by violence

[GEN 22:3](https://ref.ly/Gen22:3) · [EXO 14:16](https://ref.ly/Exod14:16) · [JDG 15:19](https://ref.ly/Judg15:19) · [1SA 6:14](https://ref.ly/1Sam6:14) · [2KI 2:24](https://ref.ly/2Kgs2:24) · [2KI 8:12](https://ref.ly/2Kgs8:12) · [2KI 15:16](https://ref.ly/2Kgs15:16) · [NEH 9:11](https://ref.ly/Neh9:11) · [JOB 28:10](https://ref.ly/Job28:10) · [PSA 78:13](https://ref.ly/Ps78:13) · [PSA 78:15](https://ref.ly/Ps78:15) · [PSA 141:7](https://ref.ly/Ps141:7) · [ECC 10:9](https://ref.ly/Eccl10:9) · [ISA 48:21](https://ref.ly/Isa48:21) · [ISA 63:12](https://ref.ly/Isa63:12) · [EZK 13:11](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:11) · [EZK 29:7](https://ref.ly/Ezek29:7) · [HOS 13:8](https://ref.ly/Hos13:8) · [AMO 1:13](https://ref.ly/Amos1:13) · [HAB 3:9](https://ref.ly/Hab3:9)

Possess

to take a part for oneself \= causative action by which someone takes part of an object belonging to someone else in order to add it to one's own possession

[2CH 32:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:1) · [ISA 7:6](https://ref.ly/Isa7:6)

Move

to burst forth, to break out \= process by which natural forces find their way by breaking through any obstacles that keep them from moving forward

[GEN 7:11](https://ref.ly/Gen7:11) · [PRO 3:20](https://ref.ly/Prov3:20) · [ISA 35:6](https://ref.ly/Isa35:6) · [ISA 58:8](https://ref.ly/Isa58:8)

Move

to break through, to invade \= action by which humans find their way by breaking through all obstacles that keep them from moving forward

[2SA 23:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam23:16) · [2KI 3:26](https://ref.ly/2Kgs3:26) · [1CH 11:18](https://ref.ly/1Chr11:18) · [2CH 21:17](https://ref.ly/2Chr21:17)

Move

to cause to break through, to unleash \= causative action by which God causes natural forces to find their way by breaking through any obstacles that keep them from moving forward

[PSA 74:15](https://ref.ly/Ps74:15) · [EZK 13:13](https://ref.ly/Ezek13:13)

Birth

to be hatched \= process by which the shell of an egg breaks open and a young animal comes out

[ISA 59:5](https://ref.ly/Isa59:5)

Birth

to hatch \= causative process by which an animal keeps its egg(s) warm for a prolonged period until the shell breaks open and a young animal comes out

[ISA 34:15](https://ref.ly/Isa34:15) · [ISA 59:5](https://ref.ly/Isa59:5)

Enio R. Mueller

## בקק (id: 001145000000000)

בקק
===

בקק (H1238)

verb

Impact

to lay waste, to devastate \= to act forcibly on something, ► so as to cause it to become waste and/or desolate

[ISA 19:3](https://ref.ly/Isa19:3) · [ISA 24:1](https://ref.ly/Isa24:1) · [ISA 24:3](https://ref.ly/Isa24:3) · [ISA 24:3](https://ref.ly/Isa24:3) · [JER 19:7](https://ref.ly/Jer19:7) · [JER 51:2](https://ref.ly/Jer51:2) · [NAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Nah2:3)

Impact

destroyer, marauder, plunderer \= someone who acts forcibly on others, ► so as to lay them waste and make them desolate

[NAM 2:3](https://ref.ly/Nah2:3)

Stage

to flourish, to spread \= process by which a plant grows luxuriantly and spreads out, often to the detriment of other plants

[HOS 10:1](https://ref.ly/Hos10:1)1

1 HOS 10:1 Already the ancient versions are divided between a positive and a negative understanding of the verb here (cf. Rudolph, Hosea, p.191\). Among contemporary versions, only NJPS ("a ravaged vine") maintains the negative understanding, which is common to this root. There is a growing consensus on a root בקק II "to flourish, spread wide" (cf. HALOT, DCH)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Isaiah 19:3; Isaiah 24:1; Isaiah 24:3; Jeremiah 19:7; Jeremiah 51:2; Nahum 2:3; Hosea 10:1

## בֹּקֶר (id: 001148000000000)

בֹּקֶר
======

בֹּקֶר (H1242)

noun m

Time

morning \= first half of the day, ranging from daybreak until noon; ≈ used figuratively to denote relief after a time of suffering, and associated with good as opposed to evil

## בַּקָּרָה (id: 001149000000000)

בַּקָּרָה
=========

בַּקָּרָה (H1243)

noun f · בקר

See

seeking, looking for \= action by which someone carefully focuses his/her eyes on a group of objects in order to locate something specific among them

[EZK 34:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek34:12)

noun f · בקר

Think

inquiry \= action by which someone gives careful thought to a certain issue in order to make an informed decision about it

[LEV 19:20](https://ref.ly/Lev19:20)1

1 LEV 19:20 RSV NRSV "inquiry" REB "enquiry" \| NIV "punishment" GNT "be punished" \| NJPS "indemnity" NJB "liable for a fine" CEV "pay a fine" \| ESV "distinction"

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Leviticus 19:20

בקשׁ

בקשׁ (H1245)

verb · בַּקָּשָׁה

Search

See

**Collocation:** בקשׁ פָּנִים

to seek (someone's) presence literally: to seek (someone's) face); hence: \= action by which humans make an intensive effort to be in the presence of another human or deity

[2SA 21:1](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:1) · [1KI 10:24](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:24) · [2CH 7:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr7:14) · [2CH 9:23](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:23) · [PSA 24:6](https://ref.ly/Ps24:6) · [PSA 27:8](https://ref.ly/Ps27:8) · [PSA 27:8](https://ref.ly/Ps27:8) · [PRO 29:26](https://ref.ly/Prov29:26) · [HOS 5:15](https://ref.ly/Hos5:15)

Speak

to inquire, to request, to plead \= action by which humans request information or a favor from someone in authority, or from a deity or other spiritual being, either directly or through the intermediary of a priest or prophet

[EXO 33:7](https://ref.ly/Exod33:7) · [LEV 19:31](https://ref.ly/Lev19:31) · [2SA 12:16](https://ref.ly/2Sam12:16) · [EST 4:8](https://ref.ly/Esth4:8) · [EST 7:7](https://ref.ly/Esth7:7) · [PSA 104:21](https://ref.ly/Ps104:21)

Associate

to seek (God) \= action where humans make an intensive effort to associate with a deity, to serve and worship him, and to get answers to questions

Bear

to hold responsible, to call to account, to seek an occasion against (someone else) literallly: to seek from (someone else's) hand; hence: \= causative action by which humans or deities lay the responsiblity for something that happened on someone else and require compensation or punishment

[GEN 31:39](https://ref.ly/Gen31:39) · [GEN 43:9](https://ref.ly/Gen43:9) · [JOS 22:23](https://ref.ly/Josh22:23) · [JDG 14:4](https://ref.ly/Judg14:4) · [1SA 20:16](https://ref.ly/1Sam20:16) · [2SA 4:11](https://ref.ly/2Sam4:11) · [ECC 3:15](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:15) · [EZK 3:18](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:18) · [EZK 3:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek3:20) · [EZK 33:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek33:8)

Engage

to try, to seek (to do something) \= causative action by which animals, humans, or deities do whatever is necessary to perform a particular event

[GEN 43:30](https://ref.ly/Gen43:30) · [EXO 2:15](https://ref.ly/Exod2:15) · [EXO 4:24](https://ref.ly/Exod4:24) · [DEU 13:11](https://ref.ly/Deut13:11) · [1SA 14:4](https://ref.ly/1Sam14:4) · [1SA 19:2](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:2) · [1SA 19:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam19:10) · [1SA 23:10](https://ref.ly/1Sam23:10) · [2SA 20:19](https://ref.ly/2Sam20:19) · [2SA 21:2](https://ref.ly/2Sam21:2) · [1KI 11:22](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:22) · [1KI 11:40](https://ref.ly/1Kgs11:40) · [EST 2:21](https://ref.ly/Esth2:21) · [EST 3:6](https://ref.ly/Esth3:6) · [EST 6:2](https://ref.ly/Esth6:2) · [PSA 27:4](https://ref.ly/Ps27:4) · [PSA 37:32](https://ref.ly/Ps37:32) · [ECC 12:10](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:10) · [JER 26:21](https://ref.ly/Jer26:21) · [ZEC 6:7](https://ref.ly/Zech6:7) · [ZEC 12:9](https://ref.ly/Zech12:9)

Know

to investigate \= action by which humans make an intensive effort to obtain in\-depth information about a certain object or event

[JDG 6:29](https://ref.ly/Judg6:29) · [EST 2:23](https://ref.ly/Esth2:23) · [JOB 10:6](https://ref.ly/Job10:6) · [PRO 29:10](https://ref.ly/Prov29:10)1

Wish

to seek \= state in which humans experience the desire to attain a certain status or condition and do what is needed to make this a reality

==========

Wish · Speak

request \= action by which humans communicate with other humans to request a favor for themselves or someone else

## בַּר (id: 001153000000000)

בַּר
====

בַּר (A1247 · H1248 · H1249 · H1250 · A1251)

noun m

Relatives

son aramaic: \= direct or indirect human offspring, either male or indeterminate, either biologically or through adoption

[EZR 5:1](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:1) · [EZR 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:2) · [EZR 5:2](https://ref.ly/Ezra5:2) · [EZR 6:14](https://ref.ly/Ezra6:14) · [PSA 2:12](https://ref.ly/Ps2:12)1 · [PRO 31:2](https://ref.ly/Prov31:2) · [PRO 31:2](https://ref.ly/Prov31:2) · [PRO 31:2](https://ref.ly/Prov31:2) · [DAN 2:25](https://ref.ly/Dan2:25) · [DAN 3:25](https://ref.ly/Dan3:25) · [DAN 5:22](https://ref.ly/Dan5:22)

Stage

at the age of ... \= state in which humans and animals have a certain age, ◄ referred to as a child of a certain number of years

[DAN 6:1](https://ref.ly/Dan6:1)

child \> part \= member of a larger group of related people or objects, ◄ referred to as a child of that larger group

[DAN 7:13](https://ref.ly/Dan7:13)

adj · ברר

Non\-Contain

empty \= state of being devoid of content

[PRO 14:4](https://ref.ly/Prov14:4)2

Land

open field \= uncultivated, uninhabited land, ► where wild animals can live free

[JOB 39:4](https://ref.ly/Job39:4) · [DAN 2:38](https://ref.ly/Dan2:38) · [DAN 4:9](https://ref.ly/Dan4:9) · [DAN 4:12](https://ref.ly/Dan4:12) · [DAN 4:18](https://ref.ly/Dan4:18) · [DAN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Dan4:20) · [DAN 4:20](https://ref.ly/Dan4:20) · [DAN 4:22](https://ref.ly/Dan4:22) · [DAN 4:29](https://ref.ly/Dan4:29)

Good

flawless \= state in which humans possess a beauty that is not marred by anything

[SNG 6:9](https://ref.ly/Song6:9) · [SNG 6:10](https://ref.ly/Song6:10)

Right

pure, flawless \= state in which an event happens entirely as desired or required, without any interference that could be considered a flaw

[JOB 11:4](https://ref.ly/Job11:4) · [PSA 19:9](https://ref.ly/Ps19:9) · [PSA 24:4](https://ref.ly/Ps24:4) · [PSA 73:1](https://ref.ly/Ps73:1)

noun m

Crops

grain collective: \= seeds of cultivated cereal; ► used as the main ingredient for bread and related food products; ≈ regarded as an essential condition for life

## בֹּרִית (id: 001186000000000)

בֹּרִית
=======

בֹּרִית (H1287)

noun f · ברר

Soap

soap, lye \= substance, ◄ probably made by mixing the ashes of certain soda plants with olive oil, animal fats, or water; ► used as cleansing agent (AYBC)

[JER 2:22](https://ref.ly/Jer2:22) · [MAL 3:2](https://ref.ly/Mal3:2)

Enio R. Mueller

* **Associated Passages:** Jeremiah 2:22; Malachi 3:2


## ברך (id: 001187000000000)

ברך
===

ברך (H1288 · A1289)

verb · בֶּרֶךְ

Stance

to cause to kneel down \= causative action by which humans cause animals to fall on their knees

[GEN 24:11](https://ref.ly/Gen24:11)

Bow

to kneel down \= action by which humans fall on their knees, in order to show respect

[2CH 6:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:13) · [PSA 95:6](https://ref.ly/Ps95:6) · [DAN 6:11](https://ref.ly/Dan6:11)

verb · בְּרָכָה · בָּרוּךְ · בַּרַכְאֵל · בֶּרֶכְיָה · בֶּרֶכְיָהוּ · יְבֶרֶכְיָהוּ

Bless

to consider oneself blessed \= state in which humans consider themselves favored by a deity

[GEN 12:3](https://ref.ly/Gen12:3) · [GEN 18:18](https://ref.ly/Gen18:18) · [GEN 22:18](https://ref.ly/Gen22:18) · [GEN 26:4](https://ref.ly/Gen26:4) · [GEN 28:14](https://ref.ly/Gen28:14) · [DEU 29:18](https://ref.ly/Deut29:18) · [PSA 72:17](https://ref.ly/Ps72:17) · [ISA 65:16](https://ref.ly/Isa65:16) · [ISA 65:16](https://ref.ly/Isa65:16) · [JER 4:2](https://ref.ly/Jer4:2)

Bless

to bless, to praise, to greet, to congratulate, to commend \= causative action by which deities grant a special favor to humans, objects, or events, or by which humans invoke a deity to do the same

Revile

to revile, to blaspheme euphemism; literally: to bless; hence: \= action by which humans use the name of God in a curse or imprecation, either addressed to humans or to God himself
