בֶּרֶךְ
=======

בֶּרֶךְ (H1290)

noun f · ברך

knee \= central area of the leg where the thigh is joined to the lower leg; ≈ associated with stability and strength

[GEN 30:3](https://ref.ly/Gen30:3) · [GEN 48:12](https://ref.ly/Gen48:12) · [GEN 50:23](https://ref.ly/Gen50:23) · [DEU 28:35](https://ref.ly/Deut28:35) · [JDG 7:5](https://ref.ly/Judg7:5) · [JDG 7:6](https://ref.ly/Judg7:6) · [JDG 16:19](https://ref.ly/Judg16:19) · [1KI 8:54](https://ref.ly/1Kgs8:54) · [1KI 18:42](https://ref.ly/1Kgs18:42)1 · [1KI 19:18](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:18) · [2KI 1:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs1:13) · [2KI 4:20](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:20) · [2CH 6:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr6:13) · [EZR 9:5](https://ref.ly/Ezra9:5) · [JOB 3:12](https://ref.ly/Job3:12) · [JOB 4:4](https://ref.ly/Job4:4) · [PSA 109:24](https://ref.ly/Ps109:24) · [ISA 35:3](https://ref.ly/Isa35:3) · [ISA 45:23](https://ref.ly/Isa45:23) · [ISA 66:12](https://ref.ly/Isa66:12) · [EZK 7:17](https://ref.ly/Ezek7:17)2 · [EZK 21:12](https://ref.ly/Ezek21:12) · [EZK 47:4](https://ref.ly/Ezek47:4) · [DAN 10:10](https://ref.ly/Dan10:10) · [NAM 2:11](https://ref.ly/Nah2:11)

1 1KI 18:42 · 1KI 18:42 MT\-K בְּרָכֹו (sg.) \| MT\-Q בִּרְכָּיו (pl., with no semantic change) 2 EZK 7:17 This expression here probably means "to run with water" \| the image is of urine running down the knees, because of fear (cf. Greenberg loc.cit.). There has also been a suggestion that בִרְכַּיִם here means penis or the male genitalia \| in this case we would have an extended meaning within the same lexical domain, and a contextual domain Liquids \| Confidence.

Enio R. Mueller

## ברר (id: 001203000000000)

ברר
===

ברר (H1305)

verb · בַּר · בֹּר · בֹּרִית · בָּרוּר

Know

to test \= action by which someone carefully examines a group of objects in order to determine whether they meet certain criteria

[ECC 3:18](https://ref.ly/Eccl3:18) · [ECC 9:1](https://ref.ly/Eccl9:1)

Dissociate

to select, to purge, selected, choice \= causative action by which someone carefully examines a group of objects in order to determine whether they meet certain criteria, by which all that do not meet those criteria will be removed

[1CH 7:40](https://ref.ly/1Chr7:40) · [1CH 9:22](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:22) · [1CH 16:41](https://ref.ly/1Chr16:41) · [NEH 5:18](https://ref.ly/Neh5:18) · [EZK 20:38](https://ref.ly/Ezek20:38)

Clean

to purify oneself \= action by which humans carefully examine themselves in order to determine whether they are ceremonially clean and do whatever is needed to achieve to become clean

[ISA 52:11](https://ref.ly/Isa52:11)

Clean

**Collocation:** זרה

to cleanse, to polish \= causative action by which humans carefully examine an object in order to determine whether it is clean and do whatever is needed to make sure that it is clean

[ISA 49:2](https://ref.ly/Isa49:2) · [JER 4:11](https://ref.ly/Jer4:11) · [JER 51:11](https://ref.ly/Jer51:11)

Innocent

to purify oneself \= action by which someone carefully examines oneself in order to evaluate one's behavior and make improvements where necessary

[2SA 22:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:27) · [PSA 18:27](https://ref.ly/Ps18:27) · [DAN 12:10](https://ref.ly/Dan12:10)

Innocent

to act with purity \= action by which someone does what is right to someone else

[2SA 22:27](https://ref.ly/2Sam22:27) · [PSA 18:27](https://ref.ly/Ps18:27)

Innocent

to make pure \= causative action by which a deity causes humans to carefully examine themselves in order to evaluate their behavior and make improvements where necessary

[DAN 11:35](https://ref.ly/Dan11:35)

Reinier de Blois

## בשׁל (id: 001207000000000)

בשׁל
====

בשׁל (H1310)

verb · בָּשֵׁל · מְבַשְּׁלֹות

Cooked

to boil \= process by which food is getting ready for consumption by being immersed in boiling water for a prolonged period

[1SA 2:13](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:13)

Cooked

to boil, to cook \= causative process by which humans prepare food for consumption by immersing it in boiling water for a prolonged period

[EXO 12:9](https://ref.ly/Exod12:9) · [EXO 16:23](https://ref.ly/Exod16:23) · [EXO 16:23](https://ref.ly/Exod16:23) · [EXO 23:19](https://ref.ly/Exod23:19) · [EXO 29:31](https://ref.ly/Exod29:31) · [EXO 34:26](https://ref.ly/Exod34:26) · [LEV 6:21](https://ref.ly/Lev6:21) · [LEV 6:21](https://ref.ly/Lev6:21) · [LEV 8:31](https://ref.ly/Lev8:31) · [NUM 11:8](https://ref.ly/Num11:8) · [DEU 14:21](https://ref.ly/Deut14:21) · [DEU 16:7](https://ref.ly/Deut16:7) · [1SA 2:15](https://ref.ly/1Sam2:15) · [2SA 13:8](https://ref.ly/2Sam13:8) · [1KI 19:21](https://ref.ly/1Kgs19:21) · [2KI 4:38](https://ref.ly/2Kgs4:38) · [2KI 6:29](https://ref.ly/2Kgs6:29) · [2CH 35:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:13) · [LAM 4:10](https://ref.ly/Lam4:10) · [EZK 24:5](https://ref.ly/Ezek24:5) · [EZK 46:20](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:20) · [EZK 46:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:24) · [EZK 46:24](https://ref.ly/Ezek46:24) · [ZEC 14:21](https://ref.ly/Zech14:21)

Cooked

**Collocation:** בשׁל בָּאֵשׁ

to roast literally: boiled in fire; hence: \= causative process by which humans prepare food for consumption by holding it close to the fire for a prolonged period

[2CH 35:13](https://ref.ly/2Chr35:13)1

Stage

to ripen \= process by which fruits or grains grow to maturity, as a result of the heat of the sun, until they are ready to be harvested

[JOL 4:13](https://ref.ly/Joel4:13)

Stage

to ripen (into fruits) \= process by which plants grow to maturity, as a result of the heat of the sun, and develop fruits or grains

[GEN 40:10](https://ref.ly/Gen40:10)

1 2CH 35:13 AYBC considers this case a conflation of EXO 12:8f, which requires roasting, and DEU 16:7, which requires boiling.

Reinier de Blois

## בֹּשֶׂם (id: 001210000000000)

בֹּשֶׂם
=======

בֹּשֶׂם (H1313 · H1314) — בֶּשֶׂם, בָּשָׂם

noun m

Trees

balsam tree \= tree of 2–3 meters tall, with small, wrinkled, three\-part leaves, white flowers, and pea\-sized red berries that have a fragrant yellow seed inside; the bark of younger branches is gray, turning brown with age; it grows in desert or semi\-desert climates; ◄ Opobalsamum; ► the tree is wanted for its resin, which appears by itself in green droplets from the stems and branches, but collectors also make cuts in the branches to speed the process; the droplets turn from green to brown, clump together, and fall to the ground, where they are collected; the resin is used in holy anointing oil, as medicine, and as an ingredient of perfume

[SNG 5:13](https://ref.ly/Song5:13) · [SNG 6:2](https://ref.ly/Song6:2) · [SNG 8:14](https://ref.ly/Song8:14)

Spices

spices, perfume, balsam oil \= generic terms for spices and aromatics, but sometimes specifically: fragrant resin ◄ produced by the balsam tree; ► used as ingredient for anointing oil and fragrant incense, and as perfume; ≈ considered very valuable

[EXO 25:6](https://ref.ly/Exod25:6) · [EXO 30:23](https://ref.ly/Exod30:23) · [EXO 30:23](https://ref.ly/Exod30:23) · [EXO 30:23](https://ref.ly/Exod30:23) · [EXO 35:8](https://ref.ly/Exod35:8) · [EXO 35:28](https://ref.ly/Exod35:28) · [1KI 10:2](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:2) · [1KI 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:10) · [1KI 10:10](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:10) · [1KI 10:25](https://ref.ly/1Kgs10:25) · [2KI 20:13](https://ref.ly/2Kgs20:13) · [1CH 9:29](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:29) · [1CH 9:30](https://ref.ly/1Chr9:30) · [2CH 9:1](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:1) · [2CH 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9) · [2CH 9:9](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:9) · [2CH 9:24](https://ref.ly/2Chr9:24) · [2CH 16:14](https://ref.ly/2Chr16:14) · [2CH 32:27](https://ref.ly/2Chr32:27) · [EST 2:12](https://ref.ly/Esth2:12) · [SNG 4:10](https://ref.ly/Song4:10) · [SNG 4:14](https://ref.ly/Song4:14) · [SNG 4:16](https://ref.ly/Song4:16) · [SNG 5:1](https://ref.ly/Song5:1) · [ISA 3:24](https://ref.ly/Isa3:24) · [ISA 39:2](https://ref.ly/Isa39:2) · [EZK 27:22](https://ref.ly/Ezek27:22)

בֹּשֶׁת
=======

Groups

daughters, women literally: daughters of a town, region, nation; hence: \= women that are inhabitants of a place, town, region, or nation, ◄ as if they were the female offspring of that place

Stage

daughter \> part \= a part of a larger object, ◄ referred to as the daughter of that object

[GEN 49:22](https://ref.ly/Gen49:22) · [PSA 17:8](https://ref.ly/Ps17:8) · [LAM 2:18](https://ref.ly/Lam2:18)

Titles

daughter, my daughter \= title used to address a woman who is regarded with some degree of affection, ◄ as if she were the speaker's child

Sing

**Collocation:** בְּנֹות הַשִּׁיר

songbirds, singing literally: daughters of song; hence, possibly: \= birds that produces musical songs; alternatively: \= the act of producing musical sounds

[ECC 12:4](https://ref.ly/Eccl12:4)

noun m

Capacity

bath \= a unit of liquid measure of which the exact quantity is unknown and ranges between 20 and 40 liters; 1 bath corresponds to 1 ephah dry measure

People

young woman, virgin \= young unmarried woman, who has never been married before, and has never had sex with a man

## בְּתוּלִים (id: 001229000000000)

בְּתוּלִים
==========

בְּתוּלִים (H1331)

noun f · בְּתוּלָה

People

virginity \= state of being a young unmarried woman, who has never been married before, and has never slept with a man

[LEV 21:13](https://ref.ly/Lev21:13) · [JDG 11:37](https://ref.ly/Judg11:37) · [JDG 11:38](https://ref.ly/Judg11:38) · [EZK 23:3](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:3) · [EZK 23:8](https://ref.ly/Ezek23:8)

Know

tokens of virginity \= signs that are to prove that a particular young woman never slept with a man before getting married

[DEU 22:14](https://ref.ly/Deut22:14) · [DEU 22:15](https://ref.ly/Deut22:15) · [DEU 22:17](https://ref.ly/Deut22:17) · [DEU 22:17](https://ref.ly/Deut22:17) · [DEU 22:20](https://ref.ly/Deut22:20)

## בֶּתֶר (id: 001233000000000)

בֶּתֶר
======

בֶּתֶר (H1335 · H1336)

noun m · בתר

Non\-Whole

piece, half \= one part of an object that has been divided an object into two halves

[GEN 15:10](https://ref.ly/Gen15:10) · [JER 34:18](https://ref.ly/Jer34:18) · [JER 34:19](https://ref.ly/Jer34:19)

Shape

cleft meaning unsure: possibly: \= state in which an object looks as if it has been divided into two

[SNG 2:17](https://ref.ly/Song2:17)1

noun m

Names of Landforms

Bether meaning unsure; possibly: \= a group of mountains with many ravines

[SNG 2:17](https://ref.ly/Song2:17)1

1 SNG 2:17 · SNG 8:14 LXX "ὄρη κοιλωμάτων" RSV NIV "rugged mountains" NRSV ESV "cleft mountains" \= \[1] \| VUL "montes Bether" NJB GNT "mountains of Bether" \= \[2] \| NJPS "hills of spices" REB "the hill where aromatic spices grow" \= בְּשָּׂמִים (see )

Reinier de Blois
